{{Monthly archived talk|2026|numbers0=yes}} == Question from [[User:EmirBola|EmirBola]] (08:13, 25 February 2026) == How do I become a great editor? --[[User:EmirBola|EmirBola]] ([[User talk:EmirBola|talk]]) 08:13, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Hi, {{u|EmirBola}}, and welcome to Wikipedia! I'm [[User:Perfect4th|Perfect4th]], and I'm dropping by to help out ClaudineChionh for a day. You can become a great editor by, well, editing! Fixing typos or finding references for articles that need more references are great ways to get started if you'd prefer to start smaller, or find an article in a category you like and try finding some references to add content. Make some mistakes, listen to the feedback, and have fun! I'll put some helpful links on [[User talk:EmirBola|your talk page]] for you. Feel free again to ask away if you have any further/more specific questions, and happy editing! [[User:Perfect4th|Perfect4th]] ([[User talk:Perfect4th|talk]]) 01:41, 1 March 2026 (UTC) == Question from [[User:MUN-lover-9|MUN-lover-9]] (18:45, 26 February 2026) == Hello, how do you create a info box --[[User:MUN-lover-9|MUN-lover-9]] ([[User talk:MUN-lover-9|talk]]) 18:45, 26 February 2026 (UTC) :Hi, {{u|MUN-lover-9}}, and welcome! I'm [[User:Perfect4th|Perfect4th]], and I'm dropping by to help ClaudineChionh for the moment. Are you trying to create a brand new infobox or use a style you've seen before? You can see general information about infoboxes and how to add existing ones to articles at [[Help:Infobox]]{{snd}}you can generally find an existing infobox for whatever you're trying to do, but if there isn't one already, you can see [[Help:Designing infoboxes|Designing infoboxes]] for information about creating one, but don't forget to check with WikiProjects for consensus first :) Feel free to drop by with any follow-up or further questions! I'm glad that you are joining the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Unreferenced articles/Backlog drives/March 2026|March 2026 drive]]! Please, have a cup of WikiTea, and go cite some articles. {{center|{{Clickable button 2|Special:RandomInCategory/All articles lacking sources|Fix a random page lacking sources|class=mw-ui-progressive}}|style=margin:1em}} [[User:Cielquiparle|Cielquiparle]] ([[User talk:Cielquiparle|talk]]) 19:04, 1 March 2026 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Jacqueline St. Joan|Jacqueline St. Joan]] (03:38, 28 February 2026) == Hello, I've edited and re-edited my article and just submitted and published it. Can you see it? What do you think? And when and how will I know if it's acceptable?? And thank you for being my mentor. Jackie --[[User:Jacqueline St. Joan|Jacqueline St. Joan]] ([[User talk:Jacqueline St. Joan|talk]]) 03:38, 28 February 2026 (UTC) :Hi, {{u|Jacqueline St. Joan}}, and welcome to Wikipedia! I'm [[User:Perfect4th]], another mentor on Wikipedia, and I'm helping out ClaudineChionh with a few questions for today. I'm guessing you're talking about the article on [[User:Jacqueline St. Joan|your userpage]]? Probably you'll want to move that to [[Draft:Jacqueline St. Joan]], which you'll be able to resubmit with any changes you make. Before you do, though, I want to caution you that creating an article, especially about yourself, is super complicated as a newcomer on Wikipedia. I usually recommend that new editors edit existing articles first to learn Wikipedia article policies through normal editing rather than having to grasp them all at once to create an article. We also have [[WP:COI|special rules around conflicts of interest]] which you'll need to look through before creating an article about yourself. After that, you'll want to add [[Help:Referencing for beginners|references]] to your article, because those are what will demonstrate [[WP:N|Wikipedia's form of notability]]{{snd}}Wikipedia articles should be based on a significant amount of coverage in multiple reliable sources. :That's a lot of info all at once, so feel free to ask any further questions you may have, and happy editing! [[User:Perfect4th|Perfect4th]] ([[User talk:Perfect4th|talk]]) 01:54, 1 March 2026 (UTC) ::How do I move my article from the user page to the Draft:Jacqueline St. Joan page, please? [[User:Jacqueline St. Joan|Jacqueline St. Joan]] ([[User talk:Jacqueline St. Joan|talk]]) 16:48, 1 March 2026 (UTC) ::Hi, Claudine--You recommended that I include references in my article. Here they are--where do they go? Inside the article or at the end? Thank you and sleep well. Jackie
{{cite news |last=Cox |first=Gail Diane |title=The Court of St. Joan |work=The National Law Journal |date=November 9, 1992}}{{pb}}{{cite news |last=Johnson |first=Rheta Grimsley |title=St. Joan—To Comfort, to Inspire |work=The Commercial Appeal |date=1984 |page=12}}{{pb}}{{cite news |title=Joan of Arc Image Fits Denver Feminist |work=Rocky Mountain News |date=November 8, 1984}}{{pb}}{{cite news |last=Gladden |first=Mendy |title=SingOut Sisters: Telling the Story of Honor Crimes |work=The Santa Fe New Mexican |date=February 11, 2011 |page=Z046}}{{pb}}{{cite web |last=Warner |first=Ryan |title=Local Author on Violence Against Pakistani Women |website=Colorado Public Radio |date=April 13, 2011 |url=https://www.cpr.org/show-segment/local-author-on-violence-against-pakistani-women/}}{{pb}}{{Reflist-talk}} [[User:Jacqueline St. Joan|Jacqueline St. Joan]] ([[User talk:Jacqueline St. Joan|talk]]) 17:01, 1 March 2026 (UTC) :::(additional reference question moved here) Hi again, {{u|Jacqueline St. Joan}}! You can simply put the information currently on your userpage into the draft instead. Your reference format is coming along, which is good! You'll want to make sure that your draft content is ''based'' on references, though, so you should have references after your sentences that back up what the content of your draft says. As for the references themselves, I can't access the offline ones, but I noticed the online radio reference was directly related to you{{snd}}can you find significant coverage in any that are secondary/not directly connected to you? It can be harder, but it's important for the notability aspect. :::Drop on by again if you have any further questions, and happy editing!    ''{{resize|88%|Send New Year cheer by adding {{tls|Happy New Year fireworks}} to user talk pages.}}'' {{clear}} — [[User:Moops|Moops]] ⋠[[User talk:Moops|'''T''']]⋡ 05:12, 2 January 2023 (UTC) == "The House of the Dead (vide game)" listed at [[Wikipedia:Redirects for discussion|Redirects for discussion]] == [[File:Information.svg|30px]] An editor has identified a potential problem with the redirect [[:The House of the Dead (vide game)]] and has thus listed it [[Wikipedia:Redirects for discussion|for discussion]]. This discussion will occur at {{slink|Wikipedia:Redirects for discussion/Log/2023 January 2#The House of the Dead (vide game)}} until a consensus is reached, and anyone, including you, is welcome to contribute to the discussion. Regards, [[User:Sonic678|SONIC]][[User talk:Sonic678|678]] 19:41, 2 January 2023 (UTC) == Andrew Mumford == Hello. I hope you are fine. Ordinarily I would agree with you, but the only evidence that he's a fellow is from his own CV. Could you please look again at this issue. Thanks. Best wishes, [[User:BoyTheKingCanDance|George Custer's Sabre]] ([[User talk:BoyTheKingCanDance|talk]]) 10:13, 11 January 2023 (UTC) :Yes I noticed that. But to survive A7, it only has to make a credible ''claim'' of importance. So if you don't think it is true, then AfD would be the appropriate process. — Martin ([[User:MSGJ|MSGJ]] · [[User talk:MSGJ|talk]]) 10:14, 11 January 2023 (UTC) == Please do not change user pages unless absolutely necessary == You just changed one of my sandbox pages to include an Authority control template (User:Eewilson/New England Botanical Society), which should not have occurred. Is this a bot you are running? – [[User:Eewilson|Elizabeth (Eewilson)]] (tag or ping me) ([[User talk:Eewilson|talk]]) 22:12, 19 January 2023 (UTC) :No bot, this was made by myself. I am updating the syntax for the template. Why should it have not occured? — Martin ([[User:MSGJ|MSGJ]] · [[User talk:MSGJ|talk]]) 22:17, 19 January 2023 (UTC) ::Well, because it wasn't in there in the first place. Why would we have authority control in user pages? Categories and such are commented out. Is authority control somehow different? It seems even common courtesy would be to let me know on my talk page and give me the option of doing it myself if it is required. – [[User:Eewilson|Elizabeth (Eewilson)]] (tag or ping me) ([[User talk:Eewilson|talk]]) 22:21, 19 January 2023 (UTC) :::It was there in the first place! I changed :::
{{Authority control|VIAF=152337746|LCCN=n81072770|WORLDCATID=lccn-n81072770|GND=5214941-9|NKC=kn20100106008}}
:::to :::
{{Authority control|qid=Q55829215}}
:::which is the same information but in the preferred format by getting data from {{Q|Q55829215}} on Wikidata — Martin ([[User:MSGJ|MSGJ]] · [[User talk:MSGJ|talk]]) 22:27, 19 January 2023 (UTC) ::::Well I'll be damned. You're right. I should have had that commented out. Well, in that case, thank you? :) – [[User:Eewilson|Elizabeth (Eewilson)]] (tag or ping me) ([[User talk:Eewilson|talk]]) 22:31, 19 January 2023 (UTC) :::::So, it's acceptable to have authority control in a user space article? – [[User:Eewilson|Elizabeth (Eewilson)]] (tag or ping me) ([[User talk:Eewilson|talk]]) 22:33, 19 January 2023 (UTC) :::::{{smiley}} It's fine to leave these templates in your userspace but adding the qid keeps them up-to-date with the data on Wikidata, and one day the named parameters will not work anymore, so I was trying to help ... — Martin ([[User:MSGJ|MSGJ]] · [[User talk:MSGJ|talk]]) 22:34, 19 January 2023 (UTC) ::::::And you did! :) Thank you, Martin. What I was thinking was that you hopped in and put something there that wasn't there. I wonder why I had it there in the first place? Well, I commented it out. I plan to finish that article... eventually. – [[User:Eewilson|Elizabeth (Eewilson)]] (tag or ping me) ([[User talk:Eewilson|talk]]) 22:36, 19 January 2023 (UTC) ==Authority control== Hello, MSGJ, I don't really understand what authority control is all about but I do tag empty categories on a daily basis and right now we have a lot of empty categories on this subject as you can see in [[:Category:AC with n elements]]. Since categories usually become empty when they held one article and that article is deleted, it's unusual to have a number of categories on the same subject suddenly become empty at the same time unless an editor has taken on a recategorization project. I see a bit of information that this might be the case on the talk page of Authority control but since I saw you were very active there, I just wanted to check in with you and make sure that this was an intentional emptying and not the work of one enthusiastic editor (which is more often the case). I hope you are having a good weekend (it is very rainy here in the NW U.S.) and that 2023 is starting off for you on a good note. [[User:Liz|'''''L'''''iz]] [[Special:Contributions/Liz|'''''Read!''''']] [[User talk:Liz|'''''Talk!''''']] 20:57, 14 January 2023 (UTC) :Thanks for the note Liz. It was intentional as these tracking categories are not being used for any active task now. It has since been questioned by another editor at [[Template talk:Authority control]] so depending on the discussion I may end up restoring these! — Martin ([[User:MSGJ|MSGJ]] · [[User talk:MSGJ|talk]]) 21:39, 16 January 2023 (UTC) ::Circling back here today, thank you for the response. Do you play on keeping [[:Category:AC with 0 elements]]? Because, as I'm sure you know, categories are used to organize content and having only one category on a subject is not typically done unless we have a habit of keeping a certain category across a lot of different parent categories (like people born in a certain year or football players from a certain country). It's worth keeping this one if it will be useful to other editors but if you are no longer categorizing pages by element number, it might be worth the effort to have a bot remove this category from the 124,844 pages it's used on if it doesn't serve a useful function. We are continually working against overcategorization with some articles (although it looks like this is a hidden category). Thanks! [[User:Liz|'''''L'''''iz]] [[Special:Contributions/Liz|'''''Read!''''']] [[User talk:Liz|'''''Talk!''''']] 00:39, 22 January 2023 (UTC) :::Hi liz, that category is gradually emptying but it's taking longer because its so large! — Martin ([[User:MSGJ|MSGJ]] · [[User talk:MSGJ|talk]]) 03:04, 22 January 2023 (UTC) == Wikidata, {Authority control} == Follow up to [[User talk:P64#LAC]]. I will have a question or three. First I see that the default display of EN.wikipedia articles has changed. After some exploration at top right I find interwiki links for DE.wikipedia and so on, but no '''interwiki link to Wikidata''' (crucial for me). I find my own talk page and Contributions. At top left I find article Contents list. I miss '''What links here?''' (useful to me) and maybe others. --[[User:P64|P64]] ([[User talk:P64|talk]]) 14:02, 22 January 2023 (UTC) :I guess you are using the new skin. You should find the "Wikidata item" link in the toolbar on the left side, but you may have to scroll to the top of the page to see the toolbar? "What links here" is in the same place (on my browser/skin) — Martin ([[User:MSGJ|MSGJ]] · [[User talk:MSGJ|talk]]) 14:53, 22 January 2023 (UTC) {{-}} == Precious anniversary == {{User QAIbox/auto|years=Four}} --[[User:Gerda Arendt|Gerda Arendt]] ([[User talk:Gerda Arendt|talk]]) 09:44, 28 January 2023 (UTC) == Definition of height of a lighthouse == Hi, can you please clarify for me the definition of height that is to apply to the Wikidata property height for a lighthouse. Looking at some of the citations and local sources here in NZ, it seems to be the industry convention that the height refers to the total height from the base of the tower to the top of the lantern. See: [https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/NENZC18700702.2.10]. This would also help to explain why Maritime New Zealand quotes the tower height of the Puysegur Point lighthouse as 5 metres [https://www.maritimenz.govt.nz/content/public/history/lighthouses/Puysegur-Point/default.asp], when from the photograph, you can see that the "tower" itself is a small concrete foundation [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Puysegur_Point_Lighthouse#/media/File:Puysegur_Point_lighthouse.jpg]. However, if there is a different definition for the height of the tower in Wikidata I will try to be consistent. Thanks, [[User:Marshelec|Marshelec]] ([[User talk:Marshelec|talk]]) 21:12, 1 February 2023 (UTC) :Hi Marshelec! I have always assumed it was the total height of the whole building (including base, tower, lantern) from the ground. In your example I'm not sure that antenna would count, but the lantern is certainly part of the building. Of course we have a seperate property for {{Q|P2923}} which I think is well understood to be the height of the actual light above mean sea level. — Martin ([[User:MSGJ|MSGJ]] · [[User talk:MSGJ|talk]]) 21:16, 1 February 2023 (UTC) ::Thanks, I am continuing to make progress with developing the article. Today I found fascinating video about the lighthouse that you might enjoy: [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xdbnRCjFztg]. Seeing this video was helpful (even though I have been fortunate to visit this remote site myself a couple of years ago). The video has prompted me to search for some additional sources on several topics, and especially to write up a summary of the climate. I might try to get this article to GA standard, but there is still a fair way to go. I do have a further question. The video clearly shows that the current light uses a modern rotating beacon installed within the old lantern house. The fresnel lenses installed when they replaced the lighthouse in 1942-43 are no longer in service. Presumably I just remove the lenses property data from Wikidata, so that it represents the current state ? [[User:Marshelec|Marshelec]] ([[User talk:Marshelec|talk]]) 07:37, 5 February 2023 (UTC) :::The best way is to add a qualifier for {{Q|P582}} to the statement, and then add another statement with qualifier {{Q|P580}} with the new value. It's generally best not to remove statements. Great work with the article, I'll come and check it out! — Martin ([[User:MSGJ|MSGJ]] · [[User talk:MSGJ|talk]]) 09:08, 5 February 2023 (UTC) == Quality assessment work group == This proposal seems almost certain to get approved. I will close the discussion after 7 days, tomorrow morning my time. We need a project organization and discussion pages to move it through to implementation. Probably there should be a temporary wikiproject to coordinate this. I could start that. Aspects I can see include: *Code, test and implement changes to {{tl|WikiProject banner shell}} *Code, test and implement changes to {{tl|WPBannerMeta}} (including support for inactive projects) *Set up a list of opt-out projects, and invite all projects to add to the list. We may want some evidence of discussion. *Design and develop a bot to move assessments up from wikiproject banners to {{tl|WikiProject banner shell}} *Deal with all the unexpected issues that will come up I have done a fair amount of template editing, but think there are other editors much better qualified than me to do the changes. {{tl|WPBannerMeta}} is quite complex. I am happy to help out with other aspects, but are you interested in taking the lead for this next phase? [[User:Aymatth2|Aymatth2]] ([[User talk:Aymatth2|talk]]) 13:53, 12 February 2023 (UTC) :We have already started discussing at [[Template talk:WikiProject banner shell]] :) That seemed the most appropriate place. I am working on WPBannerMeta, and I've asked Qwerfjkl to take the lead on the changes needed on WPBS. — Martin ([[User:MSGJ|MSGJ]] · [[User talk:MSGJ|talk]]) 13:56, 12 February 2023 (UTC) ::Ok. Good. The reason I was thinking of starting a wikiproject is that some discussions (e.g. bot, opt-out) could get quite large and may be better on pages of their own. Maybe that would be overkill though. When I close the proposal I will point to [[Template talk:WikiProject banner shell]]. [[User:Aymatth2|Aymatth2]] ([[User talk:Aymatth2|talk]]) 15:04, 12 February 2023 (UTC) :::Let's see how it goes for now. I've started using level 2 headers on that page because I think discussion will need it. I am now starting to question the whole architecture of the system, as you will see from recent posts. — Martin ([[User:MSGJ|MSGJ]] · [[User talk:MSGJ|talk]]) 21:46, 14 February 2023 (UTC) == Have I forgotten anything? == Hi. I've been a bit scattered recently; I may have lost track of things. Have I neglected to reply to anything, regarding [[Template talk:Authority control]]? How is the reboot/cleanup process going? Would my feedback be helpful? – [[User:Scyrme|Scyrme]] ([[User talk:Scyrme|talk]]) 01:23, 14 February 2023 (UTC) :Hi Scyrme, I think we are on top of everything. To be honest, I'm starting to feel that my development of this template is reaching an end as we've tackled most of the big issues. Unless you can think of anything else? There's just that loose end of how to represent multiple ids by showing the name, but I'm ready to drop that if there is further opposition to the idea. :By the way, I am gradually clearing out [[:Category:Pages using authority control with parameters]] and my hope is that one day we will be able to turn off local parameters altogether. — Martin ([[User:MSGJ|MSGJ]] · [[User talk:MSGJ|talk]]) 21:57, 14 February 2023 (UTC) == "[[:Template:Editnotices/page/Template:Wikia/list]]" listed at [[Wikipedia:Redirects for discussion|Redirects for discussion]] == [[File:Information.svg|30px]] The redirect [//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Template:Editnotices/page/Template:Wikia/list&redirect=no Template:Editnotices/page/Template:Wikia/list] has been listed at [[Wikipedia:Redirects for discussion|redirects for discussion]] to determine whether its use and function meets the [[Wikipedia:Redirect|redirect guidelines]]. Anyone, including you, is welcome to comment on this redirect at {{slink|Wikipedia:Redirects for discussion/Log/2023 February 23#Template:Editnotices/page/Template:Wikia/list}} until a consensus is reached. [[User:NmWTfs85lXusaybq|NmWTfs85lXusaybq]] ([[User talk:NmWTfs85lXusaybq|talk]]) 09:12, 23 February 2023 (UTC) == Wikipedia redirects, {Authority control} == Follow up to [[User talk:P64#LAC]] (its 2023-01-19 continuation):
Does Wikidata now welcome people items for subjects of English Wikipedia redirects who are not covered by a Wikipedia article in any language? Heavy use of template {authority control} in my user space dates from my creation and curation of many EN.wiki {{c|Redirects from people}}, such as the first 200 {{c|Redirects to joint biographies}}. --[[User:P64|P64]] ([[User talk:P64|talk]]) 19:22, 23 February 2023 (UTC) ... For instance 2022-11-17: * at EN redirect {{-r|Jerry West (author)}}: [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Jerry_West_%28author%29&diff=1122445587&oldid=778534825 "(identifiers from wikidata)"] * at Q16440448 [https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Q16440448&diff=1773689903&oldid=1514600930 "(‎Added link with badges to {enwiki}: Jerry West (author), intentional sitelink to redirect (Q70894304))"] What is the current status of this migration project?

Answer: No, I don't know how at Wikidata to add a link to EN.wiki redirect with [https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Talk:Q70894304 badge: intentional sitelink to redirect]. --[[User:P64|P64]] ([[User talk:P64|talk]]) 21:00, 23 February 2023 (UTC) ::Does this question relate to [[Victor Appleton II]]? I have created {{Q|Q116879187}} and you can try to add the sitelink and redirect badge if you wish — Martin ([[User:MSGJ|MSGJ]] · [[User talk:MSGJ|talk]]) 21:53, 23 February 2023 (UTC) ::: {{-r|Victor Appleton II}} is one instance. There I have now migrated to Wikidata all of the parameter values that I gathered at VIAF early today, Thursday here. So that EN.wiki redirect has been fixed. ::: I have created many people items at Wikidata. Now I know how to link EN.wiki redirects with '''badge: intentional sitelink to redirect''', using the faint badge icon that appeared for me at Victor Appleton II early this hour. Thanks. I am out of time today, and maybe this month. --[[User:P64|P64]] ([[User talk:P64|talk]]) 01:51, 24 February 2023 (UTC) ::::[[File:Screenshot of Authority Control template 2023-01-09.png|right|300px]] ::::Have you noticed the quickstatements link (multi-coloured arrow) which appears when parameters are present? Might save you some time — Martin ([[User:MSGJ|MSGJ]] · [[User talk:MSGJ|talk]]) 09:57, 24 February 2023 (UTC) :Yes, Wikidata contains many notable people who do not yet have a Wikipedia article. And if an item does not yet exist, then you should please create it. They look notable, but without significant coverage in multiple sources, they won't be allowed to stay — Martin ([[User:MSGJ|MSGJ]] · [[User talk:MSGJ|talk]]) 14:25, 19 April 2024 (UTC) == Possible WikiProject banner optimization == Hey, I was looking at [[Template:WikiProject Television]] and was wondering if the module can be updated to handle the code with less boilerplate code being written. So for example, a standard task force there is written like this: |TF_3_LINK = Wikipedia:WikiProject Television/The Amazing Race task force |TF_3_NAME = the Amazing Race task force |TF_3_NESTED = The Amazing Race I wonder if we can do something like this |TF_style = task force |TF_1 = The Amazing Race |TF_2 = The Apprentice UK and the LINK and NAME will be handled by the module automatically as it knows the style "task force" and it knows the NESTED name. If a specific task force needs to have a different value, then they can override TF_style with the same current style. This could also potentially work with ASSESSMENT_CAT and MAIN_CAT values. Another item that can have the same treatment is the image size. Most of the images in this template use "x20px", so instead of repeatedly adding this value, we can use TF_SIZE = x20px and for any image that needs to override it they can specify a different value. What do you think? [[User:Gonnym|Gonnym]] ([[User talk:Gonnym|talk]]) 07:14, 23 April 2024 (UTC) :Yes, good idea. We already have {{para|TF_SIZE}} (which is documented) and {{para|TF_TEXT}} (which is not yet documented!) which is possibly along the same lines you were thinking and recognises _NAME_, _LINK_ and _IMPORTANCE_. For example, you can define {{para|TF_TEXT|This _PAGETYPE_ is supported by the [[_LINK_|_NAME_]] task force and assessed as _IMPORTANCE_-importance}}. It appears that yesterday, as the result of the discussion at [[Wikipedia:Categories for discussion/Log/2024 December 7#Category:Category-Class articles]], hundreds of categories were moved from titles like "WikiProject Foo Category-Class articles" to "WikiProject Foo Category-Class pages". However, the underlying WikiProject ''templates'' which assign pages to these categories have not been changed, and so ''no pages'' have actually been moved from the "Category-Class articles" categories to the "Category-class pages" categories. I write to you because of your comment last week asking "Perhaps the bot operator could give us some indication on when these will be moved, so we can syncronise the change?" Unfortunately, the bot operator (me) is not a mind reader, and so I had no knowledge of this discussion until today. The answer to your question is -- never. The bot simply will not move a page if it has been placed in a category by a template. The only way this can happen is if the template itself is edited. --[[User:R'n'B|R'n'B]] ([[User talk:R'n'B|call me]] Russ) 20:52, 21 December 2024 (UTC) :Hi. The module ([[Module:WikiProject banner]]) was indeed updated several days ago, and pages should be making their way across gradually. We set it up so that as soon as the new category was created it would start being used. — Martin ([[User:MSGJ|MSGJ]] · [[User talk:MSGJ|talk]]) 21:43, 21 December 2024 (UTC) ::As of today, I've had a bot making null edits on all pages in the remaining "Category-Class Foo articles" categories. The ones that stay after a null edit are there because of a template that is hard-coding them into that category. {{tl|WikiProject U.S. Roads}}, as you know, is responsible for the vast majority. However, there are also some groups of categories like "Disambig-Class Foo articles" and "Redirect-Class Foo articles" where many or all of the contents are talk pages in namespace 1; if your banner template(s) is/are categorizing pages based on namespace, these are going to be treated as "articles" rather than "pages". You may need some special-case treatment for these. --[[User:R'n'B|R'n'B]] ([[User talk:R'n'B|call me]] Russ) 20:37, 14 January 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Athulyachandra|Athulyachandra]] (12:51, 19 January 2025) == I am an actress and i want to upload the authentic page of mine, Athulya Chandra. [[User:Theroadislong|Theroadislong]] ([[User talk:Theroadislong|talk]]) 22:18, 26 October 2025 (UTC) :No worries! — Martin ([[User:MSGJ|MSGJ]] · [[User talk:MSGJ|talk]]) 22:24, 26 October 2025 (UTC) == Guide to temporary accounts == Hello, MSGJ. This message is being sent to remind you of significant upcoming changes regarding logged-out editing. '''Starting 4 November, logged-out editors will no longer have their IP address publicly displayed.''' Instead, they will have a '''[[:mw:Trust and Safety Product/Temporary Accounts|temporary account]]''' (TA) associated with their edits. Users with some extended rights like administrators and CheckUsers, as well as users with the [[WP:TAIV|temporary account IP viewer]] (TAIV) user right will still be able to reveal temporary users' IP addresses and all contributions made by temporary accounts from a specific IP address or range. '''How do temporary accounts work?''' [[File:Editing with Temporary Accounts.webm|thumb|350px|Editing from a temporary account]] * When a logged-out user completes an edit or a logged action for the first time, a cookie will be set in this user's browser and a temporary account tied with this cookie will be automatically created for them. This account's name will follow the pattern: ~2025-12345-67 (a tilde, year of creation, a number split into units of 5). * All subsequent actions by the temporary account user will be attributed to this username. The cookie will expire 90 days after its creation. As long as it exists, all edits made from this device will be attributed to this temporary account. It will be the same account even if the IP address changes, unless the user clears their cookies or uses a different device or web browser. * A record of the IP address used at the time of each edit will be stored for 90 days after the edit. Users with the [[WP:TAIV|temporary account IP viewer]] (TAIV) user right will be able to see the underlying IP addresses. * As a measure against vandalism, there are two limitations on the creation of temporary accounts: ** There has to be a minimum of 10 minutes between subsequent temporary account creations from the same IP (or /64 range in case of IPv6). ** There can be a maximum of 6 temporary accounts created from an IP (or /64 range) within a period of 24 hours. '''Temporary account IP viewer user right''' [[File:Temporary Accounts demo - IP Reveal.webm|thumb|350px|How to enable IP Reveal]] * Administrators may grant the '''[[WP:TAIV|temporary account IP viewer]]''' (TAIV) user right to non-administrators who meet the [[WP:TAIVGRANT|criteria for granting]]. Importantly, an editor must make an explicit request for the permission (e.g. at [[WP:PERM/TAIV]])—administrators are not permitted to assign the right without a request. * Administrators will automatically be able to see temporary account IP information once they have accepted the [[wmf:Policy:Wikimedia Access to Temporary Account IP Addresses Policy|Access to Temporary Account IP Addresses Policy]] via [[Special:Preferences]] or via the onboarding dialog which comes up after temporary accounts are deployed. '''Impact for administrators''' * It will be possible to block many abusers by just blocking their temporary accounts. A blocked person won't be able to create new temporary accounts quickly if the admin selects the [[mw:Autoblock|autoblock]] option. * It will still be possible to block an IP address or IP range. * Temporary accounts will not be retroactively applied to contributions made before the deployment. On [[Special:Contributions]], you will be able to see existing IP user contributions, but not new contributions made by temporary accounts on that IP address. Instead, you should use [[Special:IPContributions]] for this (see a video about IPContributions in a gallery below). '''Rules about IP information disclosure''' * Publicizing an IP address gained through TAIV access is [[wmf:Policy:Wikimedia Access to Temporary Account IP Addresses Policy#Disclosure|generally]] not allowed (e.g. {{!tq|~2025-12345-67 previously edited as 192.0.2.1}} or {{!tq|~2025-12345-67's IP address is 192.0.2.1}}). * Publicly linking a TA to another TA is allowed if "reasonably believed to be necessary". (e.g. {{tq|~2025-12345-67 and ~2025-12345-68 are likely the same person, so I am counting their reverts together toward [[WP:3RR|3RR]]}}, but not {{!tq|Hey ~2025-12345-68, you did some good editing as ~2025-12345-67}}) * See {{section link|Wikipedia:Temporary account IP viewer|What can and can't be said}} for more detailed guidelines. '''Useful tools for patrollers''' * It is possible to view if a user has opted-in to view temporary account IPs via the [[mw:Product Safety and Integrity/Anti-abuse signals/User Info|User Info card]], available in {{Preferences|Appearance|Advanced options|check={{int:checkuser-userinfocard-enable-preference-description}}}} ** This feature also makes it possible for anyone to see the approximate count of temporary accounts active on the same IP address range. * [[mw:Trust and Safety Product/Temporary Accounts/Access to IP#Special:IPContributions|Special:IPContributions]] allows viewing all edits and temporary accounts connected to a specific IP address or IP range. * Similarly, [[mw:Trust and Safety Product/Temporary Accounts/Access to IP#Special:GlobalContributions|Special:GlobalContributions]] supports global search for a given temporary account's activity. * The auto-reveal feature (see video below) allows users with the right permissions to automatically reveal all IP addresses for a limited time window. '''Videos''' Temporary Accounts demo - IP Contributions.webm|How to use Special:IPContributions Temporary Accounts demo - IP Auto-reveal.webm|How automatic IP reveal works Temporary Accounts demo - IP Information.webm|How to use IP Info Temporary Accounts demo - User Info Card.webm|How to use User Info '''Further information and discussion''' * For more information and discussion regarding this change, please see the announcement from the Wikimedia Foundation at {{section link|Wikipedia:Village pump (WMF)|Temporary accounts rollout}}. Most of this message was written by [[user:Mz7|Mz7]] ([[User:Mz7/sandbox/draft temp account massmessage|source]]). {{talk archive navigation}} == Question from [[User:MercsHistorian|MercsHistorian]] (23:04, 4 March 2026) == Hi, sorry you have been lumbered with me as your student! I was wondering if I could get some advice on how best to edit the page “commonwealth winter games”…it’s full of tables, pics, links and references…only they are almost entirely wrong or lacking any detail. It also has hyperlinks that go nowhere, aren’t relevant to the games or loop back to the main page. I’ve been gathering all the records I can from local sources for the games, and I have a pretty good stub with more to come once confirmed. I’m wondering if I get an article written up in word (first) I could pick your brains on how to correct it. I tried making a little tweak just now and broke the formatting! --[[User:MercsHistorian|MercsHistorian]] ([[User talk:MercsHistorian|talk]]) 23:04, 4 March 2026 (UTC) :The article does certainly seem to be quite short and lacking in detail. What additional references do you have? Knowing those may give me an idea of how to develop the article further. :Tables are sometimes difficult to get working. There is a [[Help:Table|help page]] that may be able to guide you further. [[User:Ritchie333|Ritchie333]] [[User talk:Ritchie333|(talk)]] [[Special:Contributions/Ritchie333|(cont)]] 17:12, 5 March 2026 (UTC) ::I've made a few early changes...I think Wikipedia was erroring when I tried originally. It's not a correct, albeit basic stub. It does now show the correct number of games....but I think more work is needed. At the minute I have partial records from 1962 and 1966, incomplete results from 1958 and a list of events from 1970 but no results. The table is giving me the biggest bug bear...it's a link you can click on, only it has no associated article. Ideally, long term, I would like to "flesh" that out. [[User:MercsHistorian|MercsHistorian]] ([[User talk:MercsHistorian|talk]]) 10:31, 9 March 2026 (UTC) :{{tps}} {{U|MercsHistorian}}, you've found something quite interesting, an obviously notable series of sports events that nobody seems to have worked on. As you saw, all three of the articles on the individual years' games just redirect back to that article, and as far as I can determine, there were never articles about them. The sourcing on the three years is also poor (if anything worse than for the rest of the points sourced). On the talk page, I found questions about whether there was a Commonwealth Winter Games in 1970, and established that there was, using [https://news.google.com/newspapers Google News Archive search] (quite touchy to use; type in the lower search box next to "Search Archive" and then be sure to hit that search button. Also requires careful use of inverted commas; my search string was {{tq|"Commonwealth Winter Games" 1970}}.) The amount of stuff for 1970, which the rest of the internet, including Google Books, hardly recognises as having taken place, suggests there must be a metric tonne of news coverage of the previous 3. Plus presumably book coverage. Google News Archive is useful for pre-Internet newspaper coverage, but it's a legacy site taken over by Google and with scattershot coverage. For Australian newspapers (which tend to cover sports well), it's also useful to know about [https://trove.nla.gov.au/ Trove]. Also I couildn't help noting on the talk page that two of the medal winners featured in the articles I found have 2-line Wikipedia stubs just saying they competed in the Olympics. There are an unfortunate number of such shameful stubs on people who went to the Olympics, many if not most of whom also have distinguished records outside the Olympics. It's basically shooting fish in a barrel to expand those. (End rant.) Please, please make this stuff better. [[User:Yngvadottir|Yngvadottir]] ([[User talk:Yngvadottir|talk]]) 01:38, 6 March 2026 (UTC) ::This is brilliant sleuthing too. I'm going old school...which is making it hard to cite. I've got first hand confirmation from some of the competitors to the results....and I'm slowly piecing together the events, dates, locations and results with a hope to making the articles (even if it's just a list of results). I'm doing better on 1962 and 1966 than the other two, where I'm really struggling to find a great deal. I did encounter the same issues as yourself - the Canadian silver medallist is only mentioned as being that in passing in a random article and nowhere else. Likewise, the Australian skier - the information is wrong as I found when I cross checked. And yes, it's amazing how poor some record keeping is - Para games are worse, as we sometimes get names but no stubs, which is a problem if the female athlete marries, carries on winning and there's no mention it's the same person etc. World Games are awful too - they didn't bother recording members in team events until 1997, so I'm also tracking down athletes that are younger than me but are already wiped from history! [[User:MercsHistorian|MercsHistorian]] ([[User talk:MercsHistorian|talk]]) 10:39, 9 March 2026 (UTC)

[[File:Green check.svg|20px|alt=]] '''Guideline and policy news'''
* Following [[Wikipedia:Requests for comment/Archive.is RFC 5|an RfC]], the web archival service [[archive.today]] has been deprecated; links to the site [[Wikipedia:archive.today guidance|should be removed]].
* A [[Wikipedia talk:Speedy deletion#Retire R3|request for comment]] is open to discuss retiring CSD criterion [[Wikipedia:R3|R3]] in favour of handling such redirects through [[Wikipedia:Redirects for discussion|RfD]].

[[File:Scale of justice 2.svg|20px|alt=]] '''Arbitration'''
* Following [[Special:Permalink/1340796318#Arbitration motion regarding TenPoundHammer|a motion]], [[Wikipedia:Arbitration/Requests/Case/Conduct in deletion-related editing#TenPoundHammer topic banned (1)|remedy 9.1]] of the ''Conduct in deletion-related editing'' case has been amended to limit {{noping|TenPoundHammer}} to one XfD nomination or PROD per 24-hour period.
* Following [[Special:Permalink/1340796318#Arbitration motion regarding Iskandar323|a motion]], the ''Iskandar323 further POV pushing'' motion has been rescinded.
* The Arbitration Committee has passed [[Special:Permalink/1340796318#Motions to clean up old remedies|a housekeeping motion]] rescinding a number of outdated remedies and enforcement provisions across multiple legacy cases. In most instances, existing sanctions remain in force and continue to be appealable through the usual processes, while some case-specific remedies were amended or clarified.

[[File:Info Simple bw.svg|20px|alt=]] '''Miscellaneous'''
* Following the [[m:Stewards/Elections 2026|2026 Steward Elections]], the following editors have been appointed as stewards: [[:m:Stewards/Elections 2026/Statements/A09|A09]], [[:m:Stewards/Elections 2026/Statements/AmandaNP|AmandaNP]], [[:m:Stewards/Elections 2026/Statements/Barras|Barras]], [[:m:Stewards/Elections 2026/Statements/Count Count|Count Count]], [[:m:Stewards/Elections 2026/Statements/M7|M7]], [[:m:Stewards/Elections 2026/Statements/SHB2000|SHB2000]], [[:m:Stewards/Elections 2026/Statements/Teles|Teles]] and [[:m:Stewards/Elections 2026/Statements/VIGNERON|VIGNERON]].
* An [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Unreferenced articles/Backlog drives/March 2026|Unreferenced articles backlog drive]] is taking place in March 2026 to reduce the backlog of articles tagged with {{tl|Unreferenced}}. You can help reduce the backlog by adding citations to these articles. [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Unreferenced articles/Backlog drives/March 2026|'''Sign up to participate!''']]

I really think it's important for us to be able to make changes ourselves. One of these days you might no longer be available and then we would have to find some other expert to take over. As part experience shows, it's not always easy to do so. As for the display on the Events page, I still think it would be useful to use columns as we do on many WIR pages which include long lists. I also think that for completed years, it would be preferable to run from January to December rather than from December to January. It might also be useful to display the events for the past full year (now 2023) but offer a show/hide option for the remainder. That kind of display may be more in line with what other WIR participants would like to see.--[[User:Ipigott|Ipigott]] ([[User talk:Ipigott|talk]]) 08:59, 6 April 2024 (UTC) :::* The sandbox is just where I play around. The logic is not too difficult to follow. As for your being around "indefinitely", we initially had the same impression with our Project X experts {{noping|Harej}} and {{noping|Isarra}} but they gradually lost interest and become involved in more exciting innovations. We ran into all kinds of difficulties, perhaps the most important being our inability to maintain our membership lists. As a result we implemented a new, fully accessible approach which seems to be working quite well. As for the show/hide options, they have proved useful in our old navigation template. Now that the Events page is apparently no longer transcluded, it might be interesting to add new sections covering most successful meeetups, contests, events repeated year by year, etc. If so, it would be useful to have a general show/hide option for everything except the past year. (But are we in fact still able to add new sections to the Events page without your help?) If a specific task force needs to have a different value, then they can override TF_style with the same current style. This could also potentially work with ASSESSMENT_CAT and MAIN_CAT values. Another item that can have the same treatment is the image size. What do you think? [[User:Gonnym|Gonnym]] ([[User talk:Gonnym|talk]]) 07:14, 23 April 2024 (UTC) :Yes, good idea. All '''[[Wikipedia:Arbitration Committee Elections December 2024#Election timeline|eligible users]]''' are allowed to vote. Users with alternate accounts may only vote once. The [[WP:ARBCOM|Arbitration Committee]] is the panel of editors responsible for conducting the [[Wikipedia:Arbitration|Wikipedia arbitration process]]. It has the authority to impose binding solutions to disputes between editors, primarily for serious conduct disputes the community has been unable to resolve. This includes the authority to impose [[WP:BAN|site bans]], [[WP:TBAN|topic bans]], editing restrictions, and other measures needed to maintain our editing environment. The [[Wikipedia:Arbitration/Policy|arbitration policy]] describes the Committee's roles and responsibilities in greater detail. It appears that yesterday, as the result of the discussion at [[Wikipedia:Categories for discussion/Log/2024 December 7#Category:Category-Class articles]], hundreds of categories were moved from titles like "WikiProject Foo Category-Class articles" to "WikiProject Foo Category-Class pages". However, the underlying WikiProject ''templates'' which assign pages to these categories have not been changed, and so ''no pages'' have actually been moved from the "Category-Class articles" categories to the "Category-class pages" categories. I write to you because of your comment last week asking "Perhaps the bot operator could give us some indication on when these will be moved, so we can syncronise the change?" Unfortunately, the bot operator (me) is not a mind reader, and so I had no knowledge of this discussion until today. The answer to your question is -- never. The bot simply will not move a page if it has been placed in a category by a template. The only way this can happen is if the template itself is edited. --[[User:R'n'B|R'n'B]] ([[User talk:R'n'B|call me]] Russ) 20:52, 21 December 2024 (UTC) :Hi. The module ([[Module:WikiProject banner]]) was indeed updated several days ago, and pages should be making their way across gradually. We set it up so that as soon as the new category was created it would start being used. — Martin ([[User:MSGJ|MSGJ]] · [[User talk:MSGJ|talk]]) 21:43, 21 December 2024 (UTC) ::As of today, I've had a bot making null edits on all pages in the remaining "Category-Class Foo articles" categories. The ones that stay after a null edit are there because of a template that is hard-coding them into that category. {{tl|WikiProject U.S. Roads}}, as you know, is responsible for the vast majority. However, there are also some groups of categories like "Disambig-Class Foo articles" and "Redirect-Class Foo articles" where many or all of the contents are talk pages in namespace 1; if your banner template(s) is/are categorizing pages based on namespace, these are going to be treated as "articles" rather than "pages". [[User:Theroadislong|Theroadislong]] ([[User talk:Theroadislong|talk]]) 22:18, 26 October 2025 (UTC) :No worries! — Martin ([[User:MSGJ|MSGJ]] · [[User talk:MSGJ|talk]]) 22:24, 26 October 2025 (UTC) == Guide to temporary accounts == Hello, MSGJ. This message is being sent to remind you of significant upcoming changes regarding logged-out editing. '''Starting 4 November, logged-out editors will no longer have their IP address publicly displayed.''' Instead, they will have a '''[[:mw:Trust and Safety Product/Temporary Accounts|temporary account]]''' (TA) associated with their edits. Users with some extended rights like administrators and CheckUsers, as well as users with the [[WP:TAIV|temporary account IP viewer]] (TAIV) user right will still be able to reveal temporary users' IP addresses and all contributions made by temporary accounts from a specific IP address or range. '''How do temporary accounts work?''' [[File:Editing with Temporary Accounts.webm|thumb|350px|Editing from a temporary account]] * When a logged-out user completes an edit or a logged action for the first time, a cookie will be set in this user's browser and a temporary account tied with this cookie will be automatically created for them. This account's name will follow the pattern: ~2025-12345-67 (a tilde, year of creation, a number split into units of 5). * All subsequent actions by the temporary account user will be attributed to this username. The cookie will expire 90 days after its creation. As long as it exists, all edits made from this device will be attributed to this temporary account. It will be the same account even if the IP address changes, unless the user clears their cookies or uses a different device or web browser. * A record of the IP address used at the time of each edit will be stored for 90 days after the edit. Users with the [[WP:TAIV|temporary account IP viewer]] (TAIV) user right will be able to see the underlying IP addresses. * As a measure against vandalism, there are two limitations on the creation of temporary accounts: ** There has to be a minimum of 10 minutes between subsequent temporary account creations from the same IP (or /64 range in case of IPv6). ** There can be a maximum of 6 temporary accounts created from an IP (or /64 range) within a period of 24 hours. '''Temporary account IP viewer user right''' [[File:Temporary Accounts demo - IP Reveal.webm|thumb|350px|How to enable IP Reveal]] * Administrators may grant the '''[[WP:TAIV|temporary account IP viewer]]''' (TAIV) user right to non-administrators who meet the [[WP:TAIVGRANT|criteria for granting]]. Importantly, an editor must make an explicit request for the permission (e.g. at [[WP:PERM/TAIV]])—administrators are not permitted to assign the right without a request. * Administrators will automatically be able to see temporary account IP information once they have accepted the [[wmf:Policy:Wikimedia Access to Temporary Account IP Addresses Policy|Access to Temporary Account IP Addresses Policy]] via [[Special:Preferences]] or via the onboarding dialog which comes up after temporary accounts are deployed. '''Impact for administrators''' * It will be possible to block many abusers by just blocking their temporary accounts. A blocked person won't be able to create new temporary accounts quickly if the admin selects the [[mw:Autoblock|autoblock]] option. * It will still be possible to block an IP address or IP range. * Temporary accounts will not be retroactively applied to contributions made before the deployment. On [[Special:Contributions]], you will be able to see existing IP user contributions, but not new contributions made by temporary accounts on that IP address. Instead, you should use [[Special:IPContributions]] for this (see a video about IPContributions in a gallery below). '''Rules about IP information disclosure''' * Publicizing an IP address gained through TAIV access is [[wmf:Policy:Wikimedia Access to Temporary Account IP Addresses Policy#Disclosure|generally]] not allowed (e.g. {{!tq|~2025-12345-67 previously edited as 192.0.2.1}} or {{!tq|~2025-12345-67's IP address is 192.0.2.1}}). * Publicly linking a TA to another TA is allowed if "reasonably believed to be necessary". (e.g. {{tq|~2025-12345-67 and ~2025-12345-68 are likely the same person, so I am counting their reverts together toward [[WP:3RR|3RR]]}}, but not {{!tq|Hey ~2025-12345-68, you did some good editing as ~2025-12345-67}}) * See {{section link|Wikipedia:Temporary account IP viewer|What can and can't be said}} for more detailed guidelines. '''Useful tools for patrollers''' * It is possible to view if a user has opted-in to view temporary account IPs via the [[mw:Product Safety and Integrity/Anti-abuse signals/User Info|User Info card]], available in {{Preferences|Appearance|Advanced options|check={{int:checkuser-userinfocard-enable-preference-description}}}} ** This feature also makes it possible for anyone to see the approximate count of temporary accounts active on the same IP address range. * [[mw:Trust and Safety Product/Temporary Accounts/Access to IP#Special:IPContributions|Special:IPContributions]] allows viewing all edits and temporary accounts connected to a specific IP address or IP range. * Similarly, [[mw:Trust and Safety Product/Temporary Accounts/Access to IP#Special:GlobalContributions|Special:GlobalContributions]] supports global search for a given temporary account's activity. * The auto-reveal feature (see video below) allows users with the right permissions to automatically reveal all IP addresses for a limited time window. '''Videos''' Temporary Accounts demo - IP Contributions.webm|How to use Special:IPContributions Temporary Accounts demo - IP Auto-reveal.webm|How automatic IP reveal works Temporary Accounts demo - IP Information.webm|How to use IP Info Temporary Accounts demo - User Info Card.webm|How to use User Info '''Further information and discussion''' * For more information and discussion regarding this change, please see the announcement from the Wikimedia Foundation at {{section link|Wikipedia:Village pump (WMF)|Temporary accounts rollout}}. Most of this message was written by [[user:Mz7|Mz7]] ([[User:Mz7/sandbox/draft temp account massmessage|source]]). '''[[User:Zackmann08|Zackmann]]''' ([[User_talk:Zackmann08|Talk to me]]/[[Special:Contributions/Zackmann08|What I been doing]]) 20:33, 7 November 2025 (UTC) == ArbCom 2025 Elections voter message ==
    [[File:Scale of justice 2.svg|40px]]
    Hello! Voting in the '''[[WP:ACE2025|2025 Arbitration Committee elections]]''' is now open until 23:59 (UTC) on {{#time:l, j F Y|{{Arbitration Committee candidate/data|2025|end}}-1 day}}. All '''[[Wikipedia:Arbitration Committee Elections December 2025#Election timeline|eligible users]]''' are allowed to vote. Users with alternate accounts may only vote once. The [[WP:ARBCOM|Arbitration Committee]] is the panel of editors responsible for conducting the [[Wikipedia:Arbitration|Wikipedia arbitration process]]. It has the authority to impose binding solutions to disputes between editors, primarily for serious conduct disputes the community has been unable to resolve. This includes the authority to impose [[WP:BAN|site bans]], [[WP:TBAN|topic bans]], editing restrictions, and other measures needed to maintain our editing environment. The [[Wikipedia:Arbitration/Policy|arbitration policy]] describes the Committee's roles and responsibilities in greater detail. If you wish to participate in the 2025 election, please review [[Wikipedia:Arbitration Committee Elections December 2025/Candidates|the candidates]] and submit your choices on the '''[[Special:SecurePoll/vote/{{Arbitration Committee candidate/data|2025|poll}}|voting page]]'''. [[User:MercsHistorian|MercsHistorian]] ([[User talk:MercsHistorian|talk]]) 10:31, 9 March 2026 (UTC) :{{tps}} {{U|MercsHistorian}}, you've found something quite interesting, an obviously notable series of sports events that nobody seems to have worked on. As you saw, all three of the articles on the individual years' games just redirect back to that article, and as far as I can determine, there were never articles about them. The sourcing on the three years is also poor (if anything worse than for the rest of the points sourced). On the talk page, I found questions about whether there was a Commonwealth Winter Games in 1970, and established that there was, using [https://news.google.com/newspapers Google News Archive search] (quite touchy to use; type in the lower search box next to "Search Archive" and then be sure to hit that search button. Also requires careful use of inverted commas; my search string was {{tq|"Commonwealth Winter Games" 1970}}.) The amount of stuff for 1970, which the rest of the internet, including Google Books, hardly recognises as having taken place, suggests there must be a metric tonne of news coverage of the previous 3. Plus presumably book coverage. Google News Archive is useful for pre-Internet newspaper coverage, but it's a legacy site taken over by Google and with scattershot coverage. For Australian newspapers (which tend to cover sports well), it's also useful to know about [https://trove.nla.gov.au/ Trove]. Also I couildn't help noting on the talk page that two of the medal winners featured in the articles I found have 2-line Wikipedia stubs just saying they competed in the Olympics. There are an unfortunate number of such shameful stubs on people who went to the Olympics, many if not most of whom also have distinguished records outside the Olympics. It's basically shooting fish in a barrel to expand those. (End rant.) Please, please make this stuff better. [[User:Yngvadottir|Yngvadottir]] ([[User talk:Yngvadottir|talk]]) 01:38, 6 March 2026 (UTC) ::This is brilliant sleuthing too. I'm going old school...which is making it hard to cite. I'm doing better on 1962 and 1966 than the other two, where I'm really struggling to find a great deal. I did encounter the same issues as yourself - the Canadian silver medallist is only mentioned as being that in passing in a random article and nowhere else. Likewise, the Australian skier - the information is wrong as I found when I cross checked. And yes, it's amazing how poor some record keeping is - Para games are worse, as we sometimes get names but no stubs, which is a problem if the female athlete marries, carries on winning and there's no mention it's the same person etc. World Games are awful too - they didn't bother recording members in team events until 1997, so I'm also tracking down athletes that are younger than me but are already wiped from history! [[File:Green check.svg|20px|alt=]] '''Guideline and policy news'''
* Following [[Wikipedia:Requests for comment/Archive.is RFC 5|an RfC]], the web archival service [[archive.today]] has been deprecated; links to the site [[Wikipedia:archive.today guidance|should be removed]].
* A [[Wikipedia talk:Speedy deletion#Retire R3|request for comment]] is open to discuss retiring CSD criterion [[Wikipedia:R3|R3]] in favour of handling such redirects through [[Wikipedia:Redirects for discussion|RfD]].

[[File:Scale of justice 2.svg|20px|alt=]] '''Arbitration'''
* Following [[Special:Permalink/1340796318#Arbitration motion regarding TenPoundHammer|a motion]], [[Wikipedia:Arbitration/Requests/Case/Conduct in deletion-related editing#TenPoundHammer topic banned (1)|remedy 9.1]] of the ''Conduct in deletion-related editing'' case has been amended to limit {{noping|TenPoundHammer}} to one XfD nomination or PROD per 24-hour period.
* Following [[Special:Permalink/1340796318#Arbitration motion regarding Iskandar323|a motion]], the ''Iskandar323 further POV pushing'' motion has been rescinded.
* The Arbitration Committee has passed [[Special:Permalink/1340796318#Motions to clean up old remedies|a housekeeping motion]] rescinding a number of outdated remedies and enforcement provisions across multiple legacy cases. In most instances, existing sanctions remain in force and continue to be appealable through the usual processes, while some case-specific remedies were amended or clarified.

[[File:Info Simple bw.svg|20px|alt=]] '''Miscellaneous'''
* Following the [[m:Stewards/Elections 2026|2026 Steward Elections]], the following editors have been appointed as stewards: [[:m:Stewards/Elections 2026/Statements/A09|A09]], [[:m:Stewards/Elections 2026/Statements/AmandaNP|AmandaNP]], [[:m:Stewards/Elections 2026/Statements/Barras|Barras]], [[:m:Stewards/Elections 2026/Statements/Count Count|Count Count]], [[:m:Stewards/Elections 2026/Statements/M7|M7]], [[:m:Stewards/Elections 2026/Statements/SHB2000|SHB2000]], [[:m:Stewards/Elections 2026/Statements/Teles|Teles]] and [[:m:Stewards/Elections 2026/Statements/VIGNERON|VIGNERON]].
* An [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Unreferenced articles/Backlog drives/March 2026|Unreferenced articles backlog drive]] is taking place in March 2026 to reduce the backlog of articles tagged with {{tl|Unreferenced}}. You can help reduce the backlog by adding citations to these articles. [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Unreferenced articles/Backlog drives/March 2026|'''Sign up to participate!''']]

[[User:Goodfaithuser|Goodfaithuser]] ([[User talk:Goodfaithuser|talk]]) 20:26, 25 December 2021 (UTC) == A message from [[User:MrMatchkick|MrMatchkick]] == Thanks for suggested amendments to the page [[Three Plus Two|three plus two]] specifically regarding filming details. The comments I have so far added were those relating to off site filming in Leningrad during April-Nov 1962 as it was then rather than the updated name of St Petersburg (1991) (as the reverted page incorrectly states) . These additions were verified against the Russian version of Wikipedia for the film as well as other sites. I believe them to be accurate and helpful to aid understanding of the film. I was going to add a considerable amount of further information relating to the filming in the Crimea such as details of camera locations for the filming of the headlands walks etc. In addition I was also going to include many lesser know facts such as there were off screen relationships going on, the girls were rivals and not friends off screen and both married the same cosmonaut. The film was more successful than expected and retrospectively the actors got a payrise and so there was rumor of a sequel based in Moscow but the director died the following year due to stomach cancer etc, etc, etc ....... I've watched the film many,many,many times, during a year resyncing its subtitles from the wide screen to narrow screen format which was not an easy task. As such I feel I do know a little bit about the film and wanted to constructively share this acquired knowledge. Whilst I could spend time drafting this information , referencing and citing. All of which will help folks understand the film and its production, I have other projects off line that also require my tiny amount of spare time. As such I really don't have much more energy or the perseverance to spend working on providing information only to find that someone else wishes to change my contributions saying that they are "not constructive" . Sadly my journey with the editing of the Wikipedia project and offering further information is quickly drawing to a close, I have other places where my energies can be better spent and appreciated. I frustratingly find that many contributors have their own editorial agendas and when other information is offered in good faith and backed up, it is is not always received well as it does not fit in with a page they may have crafted in a certain way. As such I may decide only to use Wikipedia pages for reference in future I wish you well with your ongoing work with wikipedia , but I'm not sure its ethos and branding is for me. It's a shame as I feel I have a lot of valid contributions to make. No worries I still have the film to enjoy and my own private research has helped me to enjoy it considerably. It's just a shame others wont now have the opportunity to access this knowledge. Best wishes [[User:MrMatchkick|MrMatchkick]] ([[User talk:MrMatchkick|talk]]) 21:23, 25 December 2021 (UTC) :@[[User:MrMatchkick|MrMatchkick]] Looks like you were one of the few mistaken I had. I'm sorry. I've self-re-reverted. '''Recent changes''' * [[File:Octicons-tools.svg|15px|link=|alt=|Advanced item]] The Wikisources use a tool called ProofreadPage. ProofreadPage uses OpenSeadragon which is an open source tool. == Tech News: 2022-51 ==
Latest '''[[m:Special:MyLanguage/Tech/News|tech news]]''' from the Wikimedia technical community. Please tell other users about these changes. Not all changes will affect you. [[m:Special:MyLanguage/Tech/News/2022/51|Translations]] are available. '''Tech News''' * Because of the [[w:en:Christmas and holiday season|holidays]] the next issue of Tech News will be sent out on 9 January 2023. '''Recent changes''' * On a user's contributions page, you can filter it for edits with a tag like 'reverted'. Now, you can also filter for all edits that are not tagged like that. This was part of a Community Wishlist 2022 request. [https://phabricator.wikimedia.org/T119072] * [[File:Octicons-tools.svg|15px|link=|alt=|Advanced item]] A new function has been used for gadget developers to add content underneath the title on article pages. This is considered a stable API that should work across all skins. [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/ResourceLoader/Core_modules#addSubtitle|Documentation is available]]. [https://phabricator.wikimedia.org/T316830] * [[File:Octicons-tools.svg|15px|link=|alt=|Advanced item]] [[test2wiki:|One of our test wikis]] is now being served from a new infrastructure powered by [[w:Kubernetes|Kubernetes]] ([[wikitech:MediaWiki On Kubernetes|read more]]). More Wikis will switch to this new infrastructure in early 2023. Please test and let us know of any issues. [https://phabricator.wikimedia.org/T290536] '''Problems''' * Last week, all wikis had no edit access for 9 minutes. This was caused by a database problem. [https://wikitech.wikimedia.org/wiki/Incidents/2022-12-13_sessionstore] '''Changes later this week''' * There is no new MediaWiki version this week or next week. * The word "{{int:discussiontools-replybutton}}" is very short in some languages, such as Arabic ("ردّ"). This makes the {{int:discussiontools-preference-label}} button on talk pages difficult to use. An arrow icon will be added to those languages. This will only be visible to editors who have the [[Special:Preferences#mw-prefsection-betafeatures|Beta Feature]] turned on. [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Talk_pages_project/Usability#Status] [https://phabricator.wikimedia.org/T323537] '''Future changes''' * Edits can be automatically "tagged" by the system software or the {{int:Abusefilter}} system. Those tags link to a help page about the tags. Soon they will also link to Recent Changes to let you see other edits tagged this way. This was a Community Wishlist 2022 request. [https://phabricator.wikimedia.org/T301063] * The Trust & Safety tools team [[m:Special:MyLanguage/Private Incident Reporting System/Timeline and Updates|have shared new plans]] for building the Private Incident Reporting System. The system will make it easier for editors to ask for help if they are harassed or abused. * [[m:Special:MyLanguage/Community Wishlist Survey 2021/Real Time Preview for Wikitext|Realtime Preview for Wikitext]] is coming out of beta as an enabled feature for every user of the 2010 Wikitext [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Editor|editor]] in the week of January 9, 2023. It will be available to use via the toolbar in the 2010 Wikitext editor. The feature was the 4th most popular wish of the Community Wishlist Survey 2021. '''Events''' * You can now [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Wikimedia Hackathon 2023/Participate|register for the Wikimedia Hackathon 2023]], taking place on May 19–21 in Athens, Greece. You can also apply for a scholarship until January 14th. Latest '''[[m:Special:MyLanguage/Tech/News|tech news]]''' from the Wikimedia technical community. Please tell other users about these changes. Not all changes will affect you. [[m:Special:MyLanguage/Tech/News/2022/07|Translations]] are available. '''Recent changes''' * [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Manual:Purge|Purging]] a category page with fewer than 5,000 members will now recount it completely. This will allow editors to fix incorrect counts when it is wrong. [https://phabricator.wikimedia.org/T85696] '''Changes later this week''' * [[File:Octicons-sync.svg|12px|link=|alt=|Recurrent item]] The [[mw:MediaWiki 1.38/wmf.22|new version]] of MediaWiki will be on test wikis and MediaWiki.org from {{#time:j xg|2022-02-15|en}}. It will be on non-Wikipedia wikis and some Wikipedias from {{#time:j xg|2022-02-16|en}}. It will be on all wikis from {{#time:j xg|2022-02-17|en}} ([[mw:MediaWiki 1.38/Roadmap|calendar]]). * [[File:Octicons-tools.svg|15px|link=|Advanced item]] In the [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Extension:AbuseFilter|AbuseFilter]] extension, the rmspecials() function has been updated so that it does not remove the "space" character. Wikis are advised to wrap all the uses of rmspecials() with rmwhitespace() wherever necessary to keep filters' behavior unchanged. You can use the search function on [[Special:AbuseFilter]] to locate its usage. Thx! --[[User:Bronzecorvett|Bronzecorvett]] ([[User talk:Bronzecorvett|talk]]) 20:15, 28 June 2022 (UTC) :@[[User:Bronzecorvett|Bronzecorvett]] If you took the photograph, you can donate them to [https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Main_Page Wikimedia Commons] under a CC-BY-SA 3.0 license. If someone else took the photograph, you'll need to get them, or in the case of death, mental incapacitation, or being under the age of majority (that is, they're a minor), whomever is authorized to legally authorize these sorts of things on their behalf, to donate to Wikimedia Commons under the same license. Only the photographer or a legally authorized agent (their guardian, etc.) can do this, regardless of who or what is being photographed, or who is in possession of the pictures. {{MonthlyArchive}} == A message from [[User:BlakeTheGhost|BlakeTheGhost]] == A message from BlakeTheGhost Hi, Idreamofhorses. Thanks for the welcome, and for the suggestions. You've probably noticed that Mainwaring was my first foray into a Wikipedia page edit (have a long list of nonfiction authors I'd love to tackle). So I appreciate any help you can offer ... but I was highly discouraged by your having reverted the whole thing back to its original after all my research and work (learning the language to code properly in Wikitext, especially). Can we discuss the specific areas where you thought the language wasn't neutral? I aimed to state facts, used sources for (what I thought were) all my edits, corrected clear errors, added biographical details, updated (for example, there's a whole new book, so it's not true he's "best known" for that first book). In other words, I'd greatly appreciate your taking a deeper look. If there was something specifically "promotional" in there, I'm totally fine losing it (of course; as an English Professor I'm keen on getting the rules right). But ALL of it? The new book? The other career paths? The name of the business? The place of birth? I'm afraid of going forward with anyone else on my list if that stuff just gets flagged and deleted. By the way, I used Paul Polman and Paul Hawken (authors of the same stature) as models for how to do the Simon Mainwaring page. Are those equally promotional? I didn't want to start with a huge author: I thought Mainwaring was just notable enough (as evidenced by all the press, the legit clients, the book endorsements, etc.) Thanks again for engaging with a new user. BlakeTheGhost (talk) 14:27, 1 March 2022 (UTC) [[User:BlakeTheGhost|BlakeTheGhost]] ([[User talk:BlakeTheGhost|talk]]) 14:33, 1 March 2022 (UTC) :@[[User:BlakeTheGhost|BlakeTheGhost]] About you using other articles as inspiration: Unfortunately, [[WP:OSE|articles that have gone through the cracks]] exist. As for having to start over, I've seen people do exactly that and do better next time. :The lack of paragraphs in your message prevents me from helping further. (I laid out some arguments and asked some questions in the now broken-up original message). It comes down to: You reverted the entire thing when the vast majority should be OK, based on what I understand from the links you sent and the previous research I did before starting those changes. Please give me specific instances of the violation of neutral language and I'll work on those and re-revert the rest if that works for Wiki? BTW, I was following the "be bold" ethos but there's nothing bold about updating, correcting, revising, reorganizing according to norms, and adding good sources, no? Thanks again for your understanding and trying to help. All Best [[User:BlakeTheGhost|BlakeTheGhost]] ([[User talk:BlakeTheGhost|talk]]) 01:06, 2 March 2022 (UTC) [[User:BlakeTheGhost|BlakeTheGhost]] ([[User talk:BlakeTheGhost|talk]]) 01:06, 2 March 2022 (UTC) :{{reply to|BlakeTheGhost}} I wasn't complaining about adding sources, but rather a non-neutral tonality. [[File:ANEWSicon.png|right|150px]] [[File:Checkuser Logo.svg|20px|alt=]] '''CheckUser changes''' *[[File:Gnome-colors-list-add.svg|20px|alt=added|Added]] {{hlist|class=inline |[[Special:PermaLink/1115611023#2022 CheckUser and Oversight appointments: candidates appointed|Blablubbs]] |[[Special:PermaLink/1115611023#2022 CheckUser and Oversight appointments: candidates appointed|Firefly]] }} *[[File:Gnome-colors-list-remove.svg|20px|alt=removed|Removed]] [[Special:PermaLink/1115674046#Changes to the functionary team|Joe Roe]] [[File:Oversight logo.png|20px|alt=]] '''Oversight changes''' *[[File:Gnome-colors-list-remove.svg|20px|alt=removed|Removed]] [[Special:PermaLink/1115674046#Changes to the functionary team|DGG]]
[[File:Green check.svg|20px|alt=]] '''Guideline and policy news''' * The [[Wikipedia:Arbitration Committee/Requests for comment/Article creation at scale|article creation at scale]] RfC opened on 3 October and will be open until at least 2 November. * An [[Wikipedia_talk:Requests_for_adminship#RfC:_should_RfAs_be_put_on_hold_automatically?|RfC is open]] to discuss having open [[Wikipedia:Requests for adminship|requests for adminship]] automatically placed on hold after the seven-day period has elapsed, pending closure or other action by a [[WP:CRAT|bureaucrat]]. [[File:Scale of justice 2.svg|20px|alt=]] '''Arbitration''' * Eligible editors are invited to self-nominate themselves from 13 November 2022 until 22 November 2022 to stand [[Wikipedia:Arbitration Committee Elections December 2022|in the 2022 Arbitration Committee elections]]. * The [[WP:ARBCOM|arbitration]] case request titled ''Athaenara'' has been resolved by [[Special:Permalink/1116499056#Motion: Athaenara|motion]]. * The [[WP:ARBCOM|arbitration]] case ''[[Wikipedia:Arbitration/Requests/Case/Reversal and reinstatement of Athaenara's block|Reversal and reinstatement of Athaenara's block]]'' has entered the proposed decision stage. * {{noping|AmandaNP}}, {{noping|Mz7}} and {{noping|Cyberpower678}} have been appointed to the [[Wikipedia:Requests for comment/Arbitration Committee Elections December 2022/Electoral Commission|Electoral Commission]] for the [[Wikipedia:Arbitration Committee Elections December 2022|2022 Arbitration Committee Elections]]. {{noping|Xaosflux}} and {{noping|Dr vulpes}} are reserve commissioners. [[File:Info Simple bw.svg|20px|alt=]] '''Miscellaneous''' * The [[WP:CUOS2022|2022 CheckUser and Oversight appointments process]] has concluded with the appointment of two new CheckUsers. * You can add yourself to [[Wikipedia:List of administrators/Timezones|the centralised page listing time zones of administrators]]. * '''Tech tip''': Wikimarkup in a block summary is parsed in the notice that the blockee sees. You can use templates with custom options to specify situations like {{tlx|rangeblock|2=create=yes}} or {{tlx|uw-ublock|2=contains profanity}}. ---- {{center|{{flatlist| * [[Wikipedia talk:Administrators' newsletter|Discuss this newsletter]] * [[Wikipedia:Administrators' newsletter/Subscribe|Subscribe]] * [[Wikipedia:Administrators' newsletter/Archive|Archive]] }}}} {{center|1=Sent by [[User:MediaWiki message delivery|MediaWiki message delivery]] ([[User talk:MediaWiki message delivery|talk]]) 00:23, 1 November 2022 (UTC)}} == AWB adding {{tl|Lang}} error == Hi {{noping|I dream of horses}}. Recently I began monitoring {{Cat|Lang and lang-xx template errors}} and began work to clear out any mainspace pages that appear there. Within the last week or so I noticed a couple of times where there was a periodic bump up in appearance of pages (just a handful each time). It appears that your AWB task to add {{tl|Lang}} to pages is introducing an error. Specifically, {{tl|Lang}} does not allow italics markup within the template. So, when your AWB task wraps text that includes italics, for example, it generates an error message (''{{Lang|en|'' ''}}'') that is seen in "Show preview" and it puts the page in {{Cat|Lang and lang-xx template errors}}. Here are three recent instances: {{diff|title=Ion Barbu|diff=1119582518|oldid=1117744714}}, {{diff|title=Corneliu Coposu|diff=1119581079|oldid=1108096286}}, {{diff|title=Constantin Istrati|diff=1119580553|oldid=1073997061}}. For the handful that have been appearing there periodically, I or others can tweak them manually, but I was wondering if there is anything you can tweak in your AWB routine that would prevent the error from being introduced. Perhaps adding |italic=unset to the parameter if it detects italics within the text? – [[User:Archer1234|Archer1234]] ([[User talk:Archer1234|talk]]) 11:02, 2 November 2022 (UTC) :@[[User:Archer1234|Archer1234]] Yikes! That's actually a custom edit. I'll adjust my editing accordingly. Thanks for telling me! Tseng is newly deceased along with the other 2 Australian and Japanese colleagues, and has been recorded in the official archives of both states two weeks ago - though being a still-developing case, there may be more data to add later, but how many links do you expect to have been made at this moment? The only related interest side in this story is the Russian official, but do you think that they would report this person with statements or would create a Russian page for him to link with? - if so, that would be a tough task, and is not the issue of this article itself by the way, since this person's allegiance states have been covered by volunteer editors. Also the Neutrality Dispute tag remains an irony: Russian government is currently condemned and sanctioned for violating the international laws by its conducts relating to the discoveries during the unit's campaign by many states in the international community, how do you wish to be "neutral" in the Wikipedien standard in this matter? - like the patterns of Spain and Sweden did during the WWII - which actually lead to them once having hard time to join the United Nations later? - when it comes to the basic truth of being right and wrong, there is no otherwise choice but have to choose a side to believe. Please explain what you are doing, or the tag should be updated. Is it best to use Sandbox or should I directly edit a few pages first, and is there a minimum # of days I should wait before attempting to publish? --[[User:DUCharlie|DUCharlie]] ([[User talk:DUCharlie|talk]]) 21:02, 22 November 2022 (UTC) :@[[User:DUCharlie|DUCharlie]] It's a good thing you asked. If you disclose that you own/are employed at DribbleUp [[Special:MyPage|here]], you'll be allowed to write the article without risking an account block for Terms of Use violations. However, writing about your business/employer/yourself is only allowed because it's inevitable, not because it's a good idea. You could write a draft via [[WP:AFC|articles for creat]]ion, but it's probably going to get declined due to lack of [[WP:42|being noted]] and [[WP:NPOV|neutrality.]] You could do ten minor edits and wait four days to write the article directly into mainspace, but it'll likely get deleted; again, this is because lack of notability and neutrality. :The problem is, no one can think clearly about themselves, those close to them, whomever is signing their paycheck, and the businesses they compete with.
