[[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 22:15, 25 November 2024 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Edifyhub|Edifyhub]] (10:00, 27 November 2024) == Hello, I wish you could be of help for me. Please, explain to me what it is called disruptive editing. Thanks, in anticipation. --[[User:Edifyhub|Edifyhub]] ([[User talk:Edifyhub|talk]]) 10:00, 27 November 2024 (UTC) :(I hope you don't mind my jumping in, Zoozaz1 -- I was interested in this editor's activities after reading [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Administrators%27_noticeboard#New_seemingly-related_group_of_good-faith_but_deleterious_West_African_copyeditors this ANI thread], and wanted to answer an outstanding question.) :{{u|Edifyhub}}, you have been getting warnings about "disruptive editing" because some of your edits add errors that other editors have to un-do. For example, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Oradea&diff=prev&oldid=1259729022 this change] adds a mistake because that paragraph explains that Anonymus really is the name of the author. And [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Apache_Cassandra&diff=prev&oldid=1259735899 this one] adds a mistake because "throughput" is a technical term which is accurate in this context. When I look at your edit history, it looks like at least some of your edits are OK and not disruptive. [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Code_injection&diff=prev&oldid=1259740128 This one] is helpful because it fixes a formatting problem, for example. Your two most recent edits ([https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=World_Association_of_Girl_Guides_and_Girl_Scouts&diff=prev&oldid=1259910416 this] and [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Hassan_Sunny&diff=prev&oldid=1259905371 this]) also don't add any mistakes. :Please continue to be careful that your copy-edits do not add new mistakes. For example, avoid technical topics if you are unfamiliar with them, and check the context of the article to make sure something odd doesn't have another explanation. I would also encourage you to spend some time on research and adding sources, rather than just copy-edits: the English Wikipedia has relatively little need for copyediting, but there are a lot of topics that need more information added to them. There is a list of suggestions and links [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Administrators%27_noticeboard#Things_editathon_participants_can_do_that_aren't_copyediting here] with advice for non-copyediting tasks. If you have more questions you can also ask at [[WP:TEA]] or on [[User_talk:LEvalyn|my talk page]]. Thanks for your work to improve Wikipedia, and happy editing. [[User:LEvalyn|~ L 🌸]] ([[User talk:LEvalyn|talk]]) 20:27, 27 November 2024 (UTC) ::@[[User:LEvalyn|LEvalyn]] ::Thanks so much for your engagement. ::This is helpful. [[User:Edifyhub|Edifyhub]] ([[User talk:Edifyhub|talk]]) 06:35, 28 November 2024 (UTC) == Question from [[User:BigChungusFrom|BigChungusFrom]] (02:19, 3 December 2024) == Would we use American of Australian spelling? 🤔 --[[User:BigChungusFrom|BigChungusFrom]] ([[User talk:BigChungusFrom|talk]]) 02:19, 3 December 2024 (UTC) :{{u|BigChungusFrom}}, Either form is fine as long as it is consistent within the article, though unless it is an Australian topic American spelling would be preferred. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 02:56, 3 December 2024 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Djsimran72|Djsimran72]] (07:04, 4 December 2024) == Welcome to Gboard clipboard, any text you copy will be saved here. --[[User:Djsimran72|Djsimran72]] ([[User talk:Djsimran72|talk]]) 07:04, 4 December 2024 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Liikhanyeee|Liikhanyeee]] (21:06, 6 December 2024) == How can I publish my profile --[[User:Liikhanyeee|Liikhanyeee]] ([[User talk:Liikhanyeee|talk]]) 21:06, 6 December 2024 (UTC) :{{u|Liikhanyeee}}, we don't exactly have profiles here, but you can create a userpage for other Wikipedia editors to learn about you [[User:Liikhanyeee|here]]. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 21:59, 6 December 2024 (UTC) == Question from [[User:SilverJesus64|SilverJesus64]] on [[Talk:Human-powered aircraft]] (07:11, 9 December 2024) == Why is not my great Uncle mentioned in Wiki. It is my understanding, and I was at the event in 1975, North Kingstown Rhode Island at the Quonset airfield. My great-uncle lieutenant colonel Joe zinno, also an engineer along with his older brother Clarence Zinno, made US history. They have a plaque even in the Smithsonian institute stating so. Unfortunately after the flight the plane was in a hanger and both doors were open to gust of wind blew the aircraft the rope broken and smashed into quite a few pieces and was never put back together. Many people were attempting this feat to win the $100,000 prize. If you look at closely the nzb standing for Zinno Brothers.. aircraft and design and build you'll see how close the resembles the build from the MIT gossamer 3. My great-uncle was not in the best of health at the time of his peddling, but he refused to let anyone younger answer the plane and break the record. I would like you to do for the research and add my uncles my great uncle's name to Wikipedia, after all he was the first documented human powered flight in the United States of America. And your research will prove that my short editorial is accurate. He also has a short biography of his life which I encourage you to read. Thank you and God bless you your family and friends! --[[User:SilverJesus64|SilverJesus64]] ([[User talk:SilverJesus64|talk]]) 07:11, 9 December 2024 (UTC) :{{u|SilverJesus64}}, Wikipedia is open for anybody to edit, so you are free to do the research and add your uncle to the article, as long as you provide reliable, written sources - unfortunately we can't rely on personal testimony/experience, but I would recommend looking for newspaper accounts from 1975 for your reliable source. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 13:06, 9 December 2024 (UTC) ::After doing further research when I googled his name I saw an article that was containing Wikipedia already so there's no need for me to do it I was just surprised it was not in the content of the original message that I saw posted because nowhere in that particular article didn't mentioned lieutenant Colonel Joe Zinno. apparently he was overlooked in that article that I read and not included but when you look at his design of his airplane it's the exact copy of the one that won the $100,000 prize there's no jealousy here my uncle wasn't even ticked off just all those man hours building it and then having it crash and having some damage when it was flying that must have been the biggest heartache and again thank you for getting back to me so soon God bless you your family your friends in the Name of Christ Jesus amen￼ [[User:SilverJesus64|SilverJesus64]] ([[User talk:SilverJesus64|talk]]) 15:01, 9 December 2024 (UTC) :::{{u|SilverJesus64}}, excellent, glad to heard that you found that article, and best wishes to you as well. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 21:42, 22 December 2024 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Johnsv29|Johnsv29]] (13:20, 25 December 2024) == Hi I'm interested in editing an article entitled "Linkage disequilibrium". I have some experience in this field of genetics, and came on the article. I don't have problems with mistakes, just that I feel that the topic could be better explained. In fact there is a note saying "This article may be too technical for most readers to understand. Please help improve it to make it understandable to non-experts, without removing the technical details. (May 2023)" Problem is that I would really prefer to start from scratch and re-write large slabs starting at the beginning. I haven't done any wikipedia editing, and don't undertand the ethics of this. How does one do this without causing offence to the original writer(s). Thanks, John --[[User:Johnsv29|Johnsv29]] ([[User talk:Johnsv29|talk]]) 13:20, 25 December 2024 (UTC) :{{u|Johnsv29}}, no issue with causing offense to the original writers, Wikipedia's a collective project so improvements are welcome, but there are some other things you should be aware of. Firstly make sure it's written in Wikipedia's enclopedic style/tone (not like an essay with a particular argument). If you want some more guidance on this, look at some other articles on similar topics. More popular articles will give you a better sense of this, since they will be more heavily edited. :Secondly, make sure you have references. If you delete paragraphs that have references and replace them with paragraphs that don't have references, your edit will likely be reverted. If you substantially change a paragraph without references, I would suggest adding some. If you do those two things, your edits probably won't be reverted, but if they are, you can discuss on the talk page. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 20:45, 25 December 2024 (UTC) ::I've made changes to the "linkage disequilibrium" page. I hope they will be in the spirit of what's expected on wikipedia, but t's a fairly specialised topic so I assume there won't be many who will look at it. [[User:Johnsv29|Johnsv29]] ([[User talk:Johnsv29|talk]]) 00:37, 31 December 2024 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Missmarie87$|Missmarie87$]] on [[User:Missmarie87$]] (02:30, 1 January 2025) == Hi I am Melissa and I am needing some guidance/ADVICE on what to do next here --[[User:Missmarie87$|Missmarie87$]] ([[User talk:Missmarie87$|talk]]) 02:30, 1 January 2025 (UTC) :{{u|Missmarie87$}}, there are plenty of ways to contribute to Wikipedia, you can look through [[Wikipedia:Contributing to Wikipedia]] to find them, most of them involve editing and improving articles. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 00:36, 4 March 2025 (UTC) == Growth Newsletter #33 ==
[[File:Growth_team_logo_-_Icon_only.svg|right|frameless|class=skin-invert]] ''A quarterly update from the Growth team on our work to improve the new editor experience.'' === [[mw:Growth/Constructive activation experimentation|Constructive Activation Experiments]] === This year, the Growth team is exploring ways to help more new account holders start editing—and do so constructively, meaning their edits are not reverted. Our latest experiments include: * '''Gradual rollout of "Add a Link" at English Wikipedia''' – We are gradually introducing the "Add a Link" structured task to newcomers at English Wikipedia ([[phab:T386029|T386029]]). This serves as a natural A/B test to measure its impact on activation, retention, and revert rates ([[phab:T382603|T382603]]). Previous experiments on pilot wikis showed that "Add a Link" increases newcomer participation, particularly by helping them make constructive (non-reverted) edits. * '''Testing in-article suggestions for first-time editors''' – Many new account holders want to contribute but don’t know where to start. To help, we’re piloting a feature that surfaces structured task suggestions directly in an article’s read view for brand-new editors ([[phab:T385343|T385343]]). These suggestions will appear for logged-in users with no edits, providing a clear, simple way to begin contributing that is surfaced while they read. === [[mw:Help:Growth/Tools/Community updates module|Community Updates]] === Newcomers often struggle to find their place in Wikipedia’s collaborative environment. While experienced editors easily discover events like edit-a-thons and writing campaigns, newcomers often miss out. * To bridge this gap, we launched the '''Community Updates''' module for the Newcomer Homepage. This module is disabled by default, allowing Community Admins to decide how (or if) to use it. * If your community hosts events, consider setting up a Community Update to engage and welcome newcomers! [[diffblog:2025/02/12/community-updates-module-connecting-newcomers-to-your-initiatives|Learn more on Diff]]. To configure, visit Special:CommunityConfiguration. === [[mw:Community Configuration|Community Configuration]] === Community Configuration is now available across all wikis, including non-Wikipedia projects ([[phab:T383910|T383910]]). Community Configuration allows admins to customize various features like Growth features and Automoderator for their communities, and more recently the Babel extension now allows admins to modify configuration: * '''Babel customization''' – Admins can now configure Babel settings ([[phab:T374348|T374348]]), including category naming, automatic category creation, and more. See an example on [[commons:Special:CommunityConfiguration/Babel|Wikimedia Commons]]. * '''Upcoming configurable features''' – Projects exploring community configuration options include: '''[[metawiki:Incident_Reporting_System|Incident Reporting System]]''' ([[phab:T374113|T374113]]) and '''[[phab:T384444|Cite backlinks]]''' ([[phab:T378807|T378807]]). === [[mw:Growth/Feature summary#Mentorship 2|Mentorship]] === Mentors play a key role in guiding new editors. If you’re interested in mentoring, or turning mentorship on at your wiki, check out the [[mw:Growth/FAQ#Mentorship|Mentorship FAQ]] * Starting February 17, 100% of new accounts at English Wikipedia will be assigned a mentor ([[phab:T384505|T384505]]). * At Spanish Wikipedia, on 50% of newcomers get a mentor. Experienced contributors are encouraged to [[:es:Especial:Gestionar_mentores|join mentorship]] so that Spanish Wikipedia can provide a mentor to all new users. === Looking Ahead === In the coming months, we will continue balancing maintenance work—such as deprecating EditGrowth Config ([[phab:T367574|T367574]]) and migrating Statslib ([[phab:T359352|T359352]]) — with user-facing improvements that support new editors and foster the next generation of contributors. '''''[[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Growth/Newsletters|Growth team's newsletter]]''' prepared by [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Growth|the Growth team]] and posted by [[m:User:MediaWiki message delivery|bot]] • [[mw:Talk:Growth|Give feedback]] • [[m:Special:MyLanguage/Global message delivery/Targets/Growth team updates|Subscribe or unsubscribe]].''

== Question from [[User:777user888|777user888]] (15:35, 16 March 2025) == Hi, thanks for taking the time. Obvious question, but why would someone want to get really good at editing, are there skills or experiences you have come to appreciate and even become surprised from deep diving into the world of wiki-editing? -warmly, 777 --[[User:777user888|777user888]] ([[User talk:777user888|talk]]) 15:35, 16 March 2025 (UTC) :{{u|777user888}}, good question. Lots of reasons, I'd say. Firstly it's a great opportunity to improve your writing, as it's being subject to continous peer review. Practing your research skills as well can be quite useful for lots of occupations and reasons. More broadly, Wikipedia is just such a huge enterprise that there are all sorts of little niches that if you dig into can really improve your knowledge on specific topics. For instance, I've spent a lot of time working on Wikipedia's automatic inflation adjustments for currencies and on copyright law for Wikimedia Commons. For me personally it's been useful many times to actually understand how copyright works (which very few people do). From the inflation projects, I learned some basics of how to use Excel which has been quite helpful. :Wikipedia is just such a huge enterprise that if there's something your interested in, you'll likely be able to work on it. And you can do so collaboratively with other editors (which can be very helpful) for the public benefit too (which is nice motivation). It's Peter M Attia from a company called Glimpse who does battery and engineering research. It's not Peter Attia podcaster and writer about longevity and lifespan. I wanted to see what research he had done. Can you advise? --[[User:CuriousMouse2|CuriousMouse2]] ([[User talk:CuriousMouse2|talk]]) 18:09, 11 March 2025 (UTC) :{{u|CuriousMouse2}}, good catch. That template sources information from Wikidata here: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q23712954#identifiers. I would suggest either deleting the ORCID info entirely through Wikidata (you can use your Wikipedia account on Wikidata) or, if you able to find his real ORCID number, replacing the current number with that. [[User:BlinBabushka2|BlinBabushka2]] ([[User talk:BlinBabushka2|talk]]) 20:15, 17 April 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Hookemployee|Hookemployee]] (19:12, 18 April 2025) == Hello. I am a former employee of Dr Hook and Ron Haffkine. In fact I worked for Hook from 1972 until they broke up in the 80s. Ron Haffkine states that Cameron Crowe wrote the cover story to Rolling Stone's article on them when they made the cover issue following the release of their song, The Cover of the Rolling Stone. This is not true, the article was written by Tim Cahill and you can see his byline clearly if you go to the Rolling Stone Magazine archives and look up the actual interview. I hesitate editing Wikipedia myself but someone should. Ron and I had many meetings with Tim Cahill before the issue was released and Tim should be properly credited for his fine interview with the band. Ron may have been confused about Cameron because Cameron was used a lot by publicists Gibson and Stromberg. We also used Gibson and Stromberg for Hook in the beginning and both Ron and myself spent hours in their offices as did Cameron (who Ron and I met on several occasions). I guess Ron thought it was a better story to cite Cameron as the writer, but he was not. I spoke with Ron a long time ago about this but was ignored. I'm not really sure how to proceed to correct this erroneous statement. Perhaps you can suggest how I go about it? Thanks, Carla Lockhart, former Hook employee, longtime girlfriend of Ron's (11 years), publicist, secretary, music publisher, dish washer, song plugger, etc. --[[User:Hookemployee|Hookemployee]] ([[User talk:Hookemployee|talk]]) 19:12, 18 April 2025 (UTC) :{{u|Hookemployee}}, so you have two main options. The first is to post on the talk page of the article (above the article title there is a button that says "talk"), and write "{{edit COI}}", and below that write your requested edit. Make sure you include links/references to the sources that support your proposed edit. :The second option is to edit it yourself. If you do this, you have to add '''{{UserboxCOI|Ron Haffkine}}''' to you [[User:Hookemployee|userpage]] (that is a good idea even if you decide to do the first option, to post an edit request on the talk page). Then you can edit the article. Importantly, you have to add a reference (perhaps the specific issue of the Rolling Stone magazine where the interview is), just as you would for the edit request, to make sure the edit is not reverted. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 22:16, 18 April 2025 (UTC) == How notable does someone need to be to be eligible for a page. == Hi, previous reviewer said my page about Lo Cheung Kue wasn't sufficiently notable. I was wondering where the line is. He was one of the top citizens of Hong Kong in early 1900's. I have family documents which I don't want to publish and I know at least one of his living descendants has a page but not sure about linking it as they might not want that link publicised. I am happy to do more work if I am close but not if I am a million miles off. Documents from 1900 China and Hong Kong are pretty sparse. Hope you can advise. Thanks Donald [[User:DHM Wicker|DHM Wicker]] ([[User talk:DHM Wicker|talk]]) 15:48, 22 April 2025 (UTC) :{{u|DHM Wicker}}, so on Wikipedia notability is a technical term that has specific requirements that need to be met. If you look through the requirements listed on [[WP:NBASIC]], you can get a sense of whether you think you Lo Cheung Kue might qualify. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 19:05, 22 April 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:John sali23212321|John sali23212321]] (02:07, 25 April 2025) == i want to make a wiki page of my friend, how would i? --[[User:John sali23212321|John sali23212321]] ([[User talk:John sali23212321|talk]]) 02:07, 25 April 2025 (UTC) :{{u|John sali23212321}}, you can use the [[WP:AFC]] process, but first make sure your friend meets the threshold of qualification specified here [[WP:NBASIC]], and also make sure to declare your [[Wikipedia:COI|conflict of interest]] on your [[User:John sali23212321|userpage]]. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 18:56, 25 April 2025 (UTC) ==[[Wikipedia:Criteria for speedy deletion|Speedy deletion]] nomination of [[:Category:Judiciary of Sierra Leone]]== [[File:Ambox warning pn.svg|48px|left|alt=|link=]] A tag has been placed on [[:Category:Judiciary of Sierra Leone]] indicating that it is currently empty, and is not a [[:Category:Disambiguation categories|disambiguation category]], a [[:Category:Wikipedia soft redirected categories|category redirect]], under discussion at [[Wikipedia:Categories for discussion|Categories for discussion]], or a project category that by its nature may become empty on occasion. If it remains empty for seven days or more, it may be deleted under [[WP:CSD#C1|section C1 of the criteria for speedy deletion]]. If you think this page should not be deleted for this reason you may '''contest the nomination''' by [[:Category:Judiciary of Sierra Leone|visiting the page]] and removing the speedy deletion tag. [[User:Explicit|✗]][[User talk:Explicit|plicit]] 12:15, 26 April 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Jway2|Jway2]] (02:47, 28 April 2025) == Do You Only Need To Use Sub-heading 1? --[[User:Jway2|Jway2]] ([[User talk:Jway2|talk]]) 02:47, 28 April 2025 (UTC) :{{u|Jway2}}, it depends on the situation, but you can use sub-heading 2 for sub-headings within a sub-heading. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 04:04, 19 May 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Amit choudhary kalki|Amit choudhary kalki]] (16:46, 21 May 2025) == Amit Choudhary Amit Choudhary (born 26 August 2000) is an Indian actor known for his work in film and theatre. He was born in Kadila village, Tonk district, Rajasthan, and was raised in Jaipur, where he began his journey in the performing arts through local theatre. Amit pursued his early education at Rahul Shikshan Sansthan Senior Secondary School in Jaipur and later graduated from S.S. Jain Subodh College, Rambagh, Jaipur. Passionate about acting from a young age, he trained and performed in theatre productions across Jaipur before moving to Mumbai to begin his professional career. He officially started his film career on 28 October 2022 after relocating to Mumbai. Amit made his on-screen debut in the science-fiction film “Kalki 2898 AD”, director nag ashwin marking his entry into mainstream cinema. In addition to acting, Amit is an avid traveler and motorcycle enthusiast. He currently resides in Mumbai, actively working on expanding his career in the Indian film industry. --[[User:Amit choudhary kalki|Amit choudhary kalki]] ([[User talk:Amit choudhary kalki|talk]]) 16:46, 21 May 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Gods Usher|Gods Usher]] (02:26, 23 May 2025) == I have 2 questions... And I thank you for being my mentor by the way. How do I start a sandbox without it being published? Just a work station for a project without losing ground on the work that has been accomplished? 2nd question... I have been trying to add to already created pages. citing sources and such. i see that 2 things I have done has been reverted. How do I find out why? Im alright with being corrected if I do something wrong, I just want to learn and know what I did do wrong. Im very new to all of this and am trying to figure it out. i think this would make a great hobby. Anyways, thank you zoozaz1 and javahurricane. i look forward to learning from you --[[User:Gods Usher|Gods Usher]] ([[User talk:Gods Usher|talk]]) 02:26, 23 May 2025 (UTC) :{{U|Gods Usher}}, sorry for the delayed response. Your sandbox will be technically available for anyone to view, but it won't be published on the main encyclopedia for readers to view, so you can save/publish your sandbox without regular readers looking at it. :On your second question, you can press view history on any article to view the edit history, and there should be an edit summary on the edit that reverted your edit. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 10:22, 25 May 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Otheranniee|Otheranniee]] on [[Ashmina Ranjit]] (12:24, 23 May 2025) == Hello, I’m making my first edit and wanted to make sure I don’t screw anything up! The citation link goes to a 404 error page. I looked for another link to back up the statement. But can’t really find anything other links to back it up. I think this means I need to remove the whole sentence. But I also don’t believe the sentence is false because you can see the image of the work when you click on it. So would love advice. Thank you! --[[User:Otheranniee|Otheranniee]] ([[User talk:Otheranniee|talk]]) 12:24, 23 May 2025 (UTC) :{{u|Otheranniee}}, it's not formally required to remove sentences that aren't supported by a citation, particularly if you believe that the sentence is correct. What you can do is add the template {{citation needed}} right after the sentence. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 10:24, 25 May 2025 (UTC) ::Thank you! Will do. [[Special:Contributions/70.16.228.157|70.16.228.157]] ([[User talk:70.16.228.157|talk]]) 12:06, 25 May 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Phakdey chrin|Phakdey chrin]] on [[Mainland Travel Permit for Hong Kong and Macao Resident]] (05:38, 25 May 2025) == I like passport --[[User:Phakdey chrin|Phakdey chrin]] ([[User talk:Phakdey chrin|talk]]) 05:38, 25 May 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Prasanna V Dasari|Prasanna V Dasari]] (07:16, 27 May 2025) == hello, can i see my editing history which is not published --[[User:Prasanna V Dasari|Prasanna V Dasari]] ([[User talk:Prasanna V Dasari|talk]]) 07:16, 27 May 2025 (UTC) :{{u|Prasanna V Dasari}}, you have to press the publish button to save your edits - otherwise they won't be saved as edits, so you won't be able to see the edit history. [[User:Rfakjunkie|Rfakjunkie]] ([[User talk:Rfakjunkie|talk]]) 13:02, 2 June 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:SigmaKingXD2|SigmaKingXD2]] (15:37, 2 June 2025) == Hello, I love wikepidia's sigmaness! --[[User:SigmaKingXD2|SigmaKingXD2]] ([[User talk:SigmaKingXD2|talk]]) 15:37, 2 June 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Sapsaree|Sapsaree]] (16:16, 4 June 2025) == hi, i tried to edit my own wikipedia page but i am having trouble (i am 83!). in 2024, i received an honorary phd from amherst. in 2025, i received one from harvard. the news of the 2024 degree was published by amherst, and there was lots of coverage of the harvard degree. wikipedia won't accept my additions because i can't figure out how to cite them. can you help? --[[User:Sapsaree|Sapsaree]] ([[User talk:Sapsaree|talk]]) 16:16, 4 June 2025 (UTC) :{{u|Sapsaree}}, You'll have to add the citation in the edit to the article, after the sentence you add. You can do this by pressing the quotation mark towards the top of the editing screen (on the same bar that the publish sign is), and then entering the link to the article you want to cite under where it says automatic, and then creating and adding the reference. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 13:50, 15 June 2025 (UTC) == Growth News #34 ==
[[File:Growth team logo - Icon only.svg|right|frameless|class=skin-invert]] ''A quarterly update from the Growth team on our work to improve the new editor experience.'' === Mentoring new editors === In February, [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Growth/Feature summary#Mentorship 2|Mentorship]] was successfully rolled out to 100% of newcomers on English Wikipedia. Following this milestone, we collaborated with Spanish Wikipedia to expand Mentorship coverage to 70% of new accounts, with plans to reach 85% soon unless concerns are raised by mentors. ([[phab:T394867|T394867]]) === “Add a Link” Task – Iteration and Experimentation === Our efforts to improve and scale the [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Growth/Personalized first day/Structured tasks/Add a link|“Add a Link” structured task]] continued across multiple fronts: * '''Community Feedback & Model Improvements''': We’ve responded to community concerns with targeted changes: ** Restricting access to newer accounts ([[phab:T393688|T393688]]) ** Some links types were removed to align with recommendations written in the English Wikipedia Manual of Style ([[phab:T390683|T390683]]) ** Allowing communities to limit “Add a Link” to newcomers ([[phab:T393771|T393771]]) ** The model used to suggest the links was improved to ease its training ([[phab:T388258|T388258]]) * '''English Wikipedia rollout and A/B test''': We increased the rollout to 20% of newcomers, with analysis underway. Preliminary data suggests this feature makes new account holders more likely to complete an unreverted edit. ([[phab:T386029|T386029]], [[phab:T382603|T382603]]) * '''Surfacing Structured Tasks''': An experiment where we show “add a link” suggestions to newly registered users while they are reading an article is running on pilot wikis (French, Persian, Indonesian, Portuguese, Egyptian Arabic). Initial results are under analysis. ([[phab:T386029|T386029]]) === Newcomer Engagement Features === * '''“Get Started” notification''': Engineering is in progress for a new notification (Echo/email) to encourage editing among newcomers with zero edits. Early research shows this type of nudge is effective. ([[phab:T392256|T392256]]) * '''Confirmation email:''' We are exploring ways to simplify and improve the initial account confirmation email newly registered users receive. ([[phab:T215665|T215665]]) === Community Configuration Enhancements === Communities can now manage which namespaces are eligible for Event Registration via [[mw:Community Configuration|Community Configuration]]. ([[phab:T385341|T385341]]) === Annual Planning === The Wikimedia Foundation’s 2025–2026 Annual Plan is taking shape. The Growth and Editing teams will focus on the [[metawiki:Wikimedia_Foundation_Annual_Plan/2025-2026/Product_&_Technology_OKRs#Contributor_Experiences_(WE1)|'''Contributor Experiences (WE1)''' objective]], with a focus on increasing '''constructive edits''' by editors with fewer than 100 cumulative contributions. === Get Involved === We value your insights and ideas! If you would like to participate in a discussion, share feedback, or pilot new features, please reach out on the relevant Phabricator tasks or [[mw:Talk:Growth|at our talk page]], in any language. '''''[[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Growth/Newsletters|Growth team's newsletter]]''' prepared by [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Growth|the Growth team]] and posted by [[m:User:MediaWiki message delivery|bot]] • [[mw:Talk:Growth|Give feedback]] • [[m:Special:MyLanguage/Global message delivery/Targets/Growth team updates|Subscribe or unsubscribe]].''

[[User:9aija|9aija]] ([[User talk:9aija|talk]]) 12:39, 13 July 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Bakorenko|Bakorenko]] (18:31, 10 July 2025) == Hi! I am interested in working on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Causes_of_the_Holodomor The page describes one of the many famines in big developing agricultural societies and in the Soviet Union in particular. While the famines were caused by bad agricultural management and worse centralized power, the article currently tries to present a picture of some concerted and malicious attack of the Soviet Union against one of its members, the Ukrainian SSR. Communist China was also such a victim of bad agricultural practices and even more people died there. But the article is currently adamant that everything was happening on purpose against the Ukranian SSR. https://www.theatlantic.com/science/archive/2017/12/trofim-lysenko-soviet-union-russia/548786/ What can I do to make the article neutral? --[[User:Bakorenko|Bakorenko]] ([[User talk:Bakorenko|talk]]) 18:31, 10 July 2025 (UTC) :{{u|Bakorenko}}, as you probably know, this is an extremely contentious topic with people with strong POVs on both sides. If you want to change the article, I suggest focusing on specific sentences/claims, or perhaps paragraphs, that you think are incorrect, and using reliable sources (not magazine articles here, because this is very contentious, but academic journal articles or books) to demonstrate your claims. Do not try to change everything at once, but start with small specific changes that are backed up by reliable sources. If that is reverted by an editor (which it probably will be), discuss on the talk page of the article and try to reach a consensus. Repeat this process until you think the article is sufficiently balanced, and remember to assume good faith in other editors and know that this will probably be quite a long process that will require compromise. |} [[Category:Wikipedians who have received a Teahouse invitation through AfC]] == Question from [[User:Eloquentverse|Eloquentverse]] (17:39, 19 July 2025) == Hey, i have a question i am building the wikis for some early pioneers of Brazilian fintech/private equity. all of these people are super notable, one of them had sources from GQ magazine and braziljourna (oglobo) which are two huge publications, but it got declined for not being noteable enough. i am super confused. these private equity people arent exactly getting puff pieces on them, i can find interviews, i can find stats but they dont exactly have articles about how awesome they are .. i see a lot of dubious wikipedias doing the easy edits stuff of people that have shady references and just bs sounding wikis i am not understanding how my references arent qualifying. examples: Draft:Paula Bellizia, Draft:Christopher Meyn, Draft:Luiz Fraga. --[[User:Eloquentverse|Eloquentverse]] ([[User talk:Eloquentverse|talk]]) 17:39, 19 July 2025 (UTC) :{{u|Eloquentverse}}, from a brief glance, the sources you have for Meyn do help with notability, you just might need one or two more similar ones to qualify for notability. For Fraga and Bellizia, however, you'll need more, since the ones you have don't really contribute to notability. The short bios don't contribute because it's not significant coverage and the source isn't independent of the subject. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 22:16, 19 July 2025 (UTC) ::hey i redid it completely i changed some of the tone too after some feedback from others, i did some googling and found a bunch of sources on bellizia and meyn. I am also trying to build one for Draft:Francisco Bloisi the ex ceo of ifoods, but i got rejected for it reading like a CV, i am unsure now what consistutes full neutrality and non promotion when the sources use that kind of wording. i appreciate your time again seriously this site isnt exactly the easiest to navigate and i had to spend a few hours today studying requirements. letme know if it looks better! [[User:Eloquentverse|Eloquentverse]] ([[User talk:Eloquentverse|talk]]) 01:53, 20 July 2025 (UTC) :::{{U|Eloquentverse}}, I can say that you're on the right track, and Wikipedia is definitely not easy for beginners. The Bloisi article looks relatively fine to me. One thing I'll note is that sometimes, people just aren't notable. As I'm sure you've discovered, notability is a technical term with specific requirements on Wikipedia, so even if they are 'notable' in the usual way you use the term, they might not be notable on Wikipedia. That said, Bloisi and Meyn do look notable to me with the articles you've added (though note that press releases, because they aren't independent of the subject, don't contribute to notability, though of course they can be used to source information). [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 14:16, 20 July 2025 (UTC) ::::got it. ill review those two again. I had a question on Draft:Paula Bellizia, shes incredibly notable just one google search will show a ton of stuff on her but im still getting marked as not notable enough. Do you know what I am doing wrong?? Also i talked to Grazie, she thought it was AI because I misquoted a reference lazily :/ i did apologize and clean it up [[User:Eloquentverse|Eloquentverse]] ([[User talk:Eloquentverse|talk]]) 14:18, 20 July 2025 (UTC) :::::{{u|Eloquentverse}}, That's probably because most of the sources you have don't contribute to notability. Press releases - because they aren't independent, short bios - because they usually aren't independent and don't constitute significant coverage, or relatively brief mentions in articles (such as in https://mexico-now.com/amazon-announces-investment-of-us5-billion-in-queretaro/) - because they don't constitute significant coverage, don't add to notability. Once you eliminate those sources, most of the references don't qualify for notability. While iirc technically notability is independent of the references you use in the article, in reality you need to put the references that make her notable in the article - I would perhaps suggest highlighting 3 or 4 that are from reliable, independent sources with significant coverage for the reviewer as a comment. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 14:23, 20 July 2025 (UTC) ::::::Ah gotcha. I will look for sources that are talking about her specifically. Would company websites work? [https://www.weforum.org/people/paula-bellizia/ Paula Bellizia | World Economic Forum] like could I use WEF's site as a source for some of her bio? [[User:Eloquentverse|Eloquentverse]] ([[User talk:Eloquentverse|talk]]) 14:25, 20 July 2025 (UTC) :::::::I also in that same note, had a question on Draft:Amaury Bier, I did use some stuff from Gavea's website to source his bio I was however able to search the web in Portuguese and find some other stuff [[User:Eloquentverse|Eloquentverse]] ([[User talk:Eloquentverse|talk]]) 14:26, 20 July 2025 (UTC) ::::::::{{u|Eloquentverse}}, Fine to use the WEF as a source, but it probably won't contribute much to notability, since it's pretty short and probably not independent as she participates in the WEF, I would assume. No need for the sources to be in English, any language will contribute to notability as long as the source meets the requirements. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 22:05, 4 August 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Vigilante eye 1|Vigilante eye 1]] (09:23, 5 August 2025) == id light to put protection on a particular page how do i go about it --[[User:Vigilante eye 1|Vigilante eye 1]] ([[User talk:Vigilante eye 1|talk]]) 09:23, 5 August 2025 (UTC) :{{re|Vigilante eye 1}} You can't yourself but you can make a request at [[WP:RFPP]] if the page is being vandalized etc. [[User:TheSandDoctor|TheSandDoctor]] [[User talk:TheSandDoctor|Talk]] 19:23, 5 August 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Jennyrendel|Jennyrendel]] (18:46, 5 August 2025) == Hello I added my details to the list of south african artists but I don't know how to place it under the letter S --[[User:Jennyrendel|Jennyrendel]] ([[User talk:Jennyrendel|talk]]) 18:46, 5 August 2025 (UTC) :{{re|Jennyrendel}} Entries in lists like that should be supported by [[WP:RS|reliable]], [[WP:SECONDARY|secondary]] sources at a minimum and ideally have a Wikipedia article about them already prior to adding those entries to lists. Wikipedia lists etc aren't just for anyone to add themselves to; Wikipedia is an encyclopedia, not a method for [[WP:PROMO|self-promotion]]. I’ll take your comments into account and make the necessary changes before submitting it for review. ::Best regards, ::Muiliana [[User:Muiliana|Muiliana]] ([[User talk:Muiliana|talk]]) 04:23, 7 August 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:นาย มะรอฟี ดาาโอะ|นาย มะรอฟี ดาาโอะ]] on [[Special:Watchlist]] (07:07, 7 August 2025) == ชื่อ นาย มะรอฟี ดาราโอะ --[[User:นาย มะรอฟี ดาาโอะ|นาย มะรอฟี ดาาโอะ]] ([[User talk:นาย มะรอฟี ดาาโอะ|talk]]) 07:07, 7 August 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:TeenX808|TeenX808]] (04:28, 7 August 2025) == Hi I’m having a sort of edit war with an another user in Parantaka II article, he’s purposely misleading some statements and citing info which isn’t accepted , are you able to help sought the problem if yes I would highly appreciate thanks [[User:TeenX808|TeenX808]] ([[User talk:TeenX808|talk]]) 04:28, 7 August 2025 (UTC) :{{u|TeenX808}}, I'm going to be honest, looking at the talk page, I think the other editor is correct. There's a lot of evidence the other editor found saying that the Sri Lankas joined the battle against the Cholas, and the evidence you added doesn't seem to counter that. Regardless, nobody should be accusing people of lying (which both of you did) - make sure the discussion is civil. Can you please help me? --[[User:TheHistorianEditor|TheHistorianEditor]] ([[User talk:TheHistorianEditor|talk]]) 03:43, 11 September 2025 (UTC) :{{u|TheHistorianEditor}}, sure, but feel free to [[WP:Be bold]] when you make edits. When you are making edits, make sure to always include [[Wikipedia:RS|reliable sources]] that back up what you're writing, make sure to conform to the [[Wikipedia:MOS|general style of a Wikipedia article]], and leave descriptions of what/why you edited in the edit summary. Don't worry if your edit is reverted by another editor; instead of re-doing the edit, you should discuss civilly with the other editor on the talk page of the article. Those are just some common tips for editing, but I recommend that you learn by doing and listen to feedback along the way. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 23:32, 11 September 2025 (UTC) ::Can you give me some figures on the Wikpiedia, and how do amend it? I'm new. Make sure you use the visual editor, which makes editing much easier. If you want to add references, use the quotation marks at the top of the screen. There's also a guide for beginners [[Help:Getting started|here]] that you can look through. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 00:46, 12 September 2025 (UTC) ::::Can you give me links to American figures so I can edit and fix them. Tell me what detail to edit and change. Thank you [[User:TheHistorianEditor|TheHistorianEditor]] ([[User talk:TheHistorianEditor|talk]]) 21:18, 12 September 2025 (UTC) :::::{{U|TheHistorianEditor}}, you can look through [[Wikipedia:WikiProject United States/To do]] for a lot of articles that can be worked on. Specifically, you can look through [[:Category:United States articles needing attention]] for a long list of articles that need fixing for various reasons, which should be listed on the talk page or the page of the article. Many of them involve adding photographs to articles, and if you want a guide on how to do that, check out [[Help:Introduction to images with VisualEditor/1]]. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 00:59, 13 September 2025 (UTC) == Cite Unseen September 2025 updates ==
Hello! Thank you for using '''[[:m:Meta:Cite Unseen|Cite Unseen]]'''. We are excited to share details about a big update we just deployed. With grant support from [https://wikimedia.ch/ Wikimedia CH], we've added several new features, including a citation filtering dashboard, settings dialog, support for localization, and the ability to easily suggest domain categorizations. Cite Unseen now also lives on Meta Wiki, as part of our effort to serve all Wikimedia projects. Our [[:m:Meta:Cite_Unseen/sources|source lists]] are now also on Meta-Wiki, where they can be collaboratively edited by the community. Please see our '''[[:m:Meta:Cite Unseen/Newsletter/2025-09-14|newsletter on Meta-Wiki]]''' for full details. If you have feature ideas, notice any issues with our new updates, or have any questions, please get in touch via our [[:m:Meta_talk:Cite_Unseen|project talk page]]. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 01:38, 30 September 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:TheHistorianEditor|TheHistorianEditor]] (19:13, 1 October 2025) == Hello. I am scared. What should I do? --[[User:TheHistorianEditor|TheHistorianEditor]] ([[User talk:TheHistorianEditor|talk]]) 19:13, 1 October 2025 (UTC) :{{U|TheHistorianEditor}}, I see you have been blocked, unfortunately. I would recommend trying to understand why you were blocked, and then submit an unblock request in a few months as recommended on your talk page. Keep in mind that being blocked isn't the end of the world, and you should submit an unblock request in a few months if you want to keep editing and know exactly how you need to change your editing. [[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 19:27, 21 October 2025 (UTC) == Growth News #35 ==
[[File:Growth team logo - Icon only.svg|right|frameless|class=skin-invert]] ''A quarterly update from the Growth team on our work to improve the new editor experience.'' === New releases === ==== English Wikipedia gets "Add a Link" Structured Task ==== We [[w:Wikipedia_talk:Growth_Team_features#"Add_a_link"_experiment_and_next_steps|released]] the [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Growth/Personalized first day/Structured tasks/Add a link|"Add a Link" Structured Task]] to 100% of accounts at English Wikipedia on Tuesday, September 2nd (before then it was available to 20% of accounts). ==== Growth features for Wikidata ==== After examining if the Growth features and Mentorship could be adapted to Wikidata, we activated the Growth features on [https://www.wikidata.beta.wmcloud.org/wiki/Wikidata:Main_Page Beta Wikidata] to allow for testing and discussion ([[phab:T400937|T400937]]). Although some features, like Suggested Edits, are Wikipedia-specific, the Growth team designed most features to be more wiki-agnostic. === Work in progress === ==== Revise Tone Structured Task ==== The Growth team is making progress on [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Growth/Revise Tone|the technical architecture, onboarding design, and early user testing]]. We are targeting an A/B test before the end of this year, with constructive edits by newcomers as the primary success metric. ==== Add a link to more wikis ==== The machine learning team has been working on a new model that can suggest links to more languages, including Urdu, Chinese, and Japanese Wikipedias. [[phab:T404460|We are starting to release]] the “Add a Link” feature to Wikipedias that weren’t supported by the previous model. [[mw:Help:Growth/Tools/Add a link|Add a link]], which can be [[mw:Help:Growth/Tools/Add_a_link#Configuration|configured by the community locally]], increases the chance that a new contributor will make their first edit and then continue to participate in Wikipedia. === Research === The Growth team is involved in several research initiatives to help guide our future work: [[mw:Growth/Progression System|Progression System]] – We have [[mw:Growth/Progression_System#Design_Research|published]] initial findings from interviews with 10 English and French Wikipedia newcomers. The research examined motivations, challenges, and feedback on a prototype system intended to help editors build confidence, develop skills, and contribute more constructively over time. [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Contributors/Mobile Web Editing Research|Mobile Web Editing Research]] – This project combines quantitative and qualitative data, community feedback, and user journey analysis to identify possible ways to enhance the mobile editing experience. [[m:Special:MyLanguage/Research:Successful Newcomers Survey 2025|Newcomers Survey]] – This project surveys successful newcomers on English Wikipedia to understand their early editing experiences, tool use, and community interactions. === Community events === The Growth team participated in several community events to listen, share, and collaborate on improving newcomer experiences across Wikimedia projects. '''Wikimania - [https://wikimedia.eventyay.com/talk/wikimania2025/talk/RPBSEF/ Organizers as key partners to support newcomers' growth in our movement]''' This session invited organizers to share how they introduce newcomers to Growth features and the challenges they encounter. The discussion focused on common newcomer questions and opportunities to strengthen collaboration in supporting new editors. '''Wikimania - [https://wikimedia.eventyay.com/talk/wikimania2025/talk/review/VCSCUVJCGFRNY89TTKXRVFMGYMWPMSXT Lightning Talk: Structured Tasks]''' This talk demonstrated how Structured Tasks help newcomers take their first successful steps on Wikipedia. It shared impact data, community configurations, and a demo of “Add a Link,” illustrating how these tasks make editing more accessible and sustainable, particularly for mobile contributors. '''Wikimania - [https://wikimedia.eventyay.com/talk/wikimania2025/talk/TVCVAB/ Building a Sustainable Future for Wikimedia Contributors]''' With active editor numbers declining, the [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Contributors/Strategy|Contributors Strategy]] aims to create a clearer, more engaging path for participation. This session, led by the WMF Contributors group with involvement from the Editing, Growth, Moderator Tools, and Connection (formerly Campaigns) teams, highlighted efforts to streamline contributor experiences, offer structured and mobile-friendly workflows, and foster meaningful engagement. Participants learned about ongoing initiatives and shared feedback to help shape a more inclusive and sustainable future for Wikimedia contributors. '''CEE Meeting - [[metawiki:Wikimedia_CEE_Meeting_2025/Submissions/Retaining_beginners_and_improving_content_moderation:_an_inclusive_and_sustainable_future_for_Wikipedia_contributors|Retaining beginners and improving content moderation: an inclusive and sustainable future for Wikipedia contributors]]''' Many communities face a decline in volunteer engagement. Newcomers often leave soon after joining, while experienced editors struggle to manage increasingly complex workflows and overwhelming backlogs. We presented the [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Contributors/Strategy|Contributors Strategy]] and the different features and workflows that can help communities to address these challenges. We listened to the specific needs of the CEE communities to help guide the Contributors teams' work. '''''[[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Growth/Newsletters|Growth team's newsletter]]''' prepared by [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Growth|the Growth team]] and posted by [[m:User:MediaWiki message delivery|bot]] • [[mw:Talk:Growth|Give feedback]] • [[m:Special:MyLanguage/Global message delivery/Targets/Growth team updates|Subscribe or unsubscribe]].''

[[User:Zoozaz1|Zoozaz1]] ([[User talk:Zoozaz1#top|talk]]) 11:28, 12 November 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Lebootimes|Lebootimes]] (17:39, 13 November 2025) == Hi! I could use your guidance regarding the article you previously reviewed while it was still in AfC. I moved the draft to mainspace before the AfC process finished, and another editor later removed several of the sources that were originally present. After that cleanup, the same editor nominated the article for deletion (AfD). Some of the removed sources were independent Lebanese news outlets (Addiyar, Beirut Tribune, SpotLeb, LebTalks, Bimawdou3ia), which appear to provide original journalistic coverage and not PR material. Because they were removed right before the AfD, the article now looks less supported. Since you are familiar with the draft from AfC, I want to ask: What improvements should I focus on during the AfD discussion to address the concerns? Thank you—I’m trying to follow policy correctly and improve the article as needed. --[[User:Lebootimes|Lebootimes]] ([[User talk:Lebootimes|talk]]) 17:39, 13 November 2025 (UTC) :{{u|Lebootimes}}, hmm, note that notability doesn't depend on the sources cited in the article exactly, but rather the existence of certain sources to demonstrate notability - so if those sources meet the criteria, you could mention them in AfD debate. I think you've presented your case in the AfD discussion, so you'll just have to wait and see what other editors think. At the end of the day, if the subject doesn't meet our notability critereon, then unfortunately it will be deleted regardless of changes you make to the article, at least until more coverage appears. 21:14, 29 November 2021 (UTC) == Administrators' newsletter – December 2021 == [[Wikipedia:Administrators' newsletter|News and updates for administrators]] from the past month (November 2021). [[File:ANEWSicon.png|right|150px]] [[File:Wikipedia Administrator.svg|20px|alt=]] '''Administrator changes''' :[[File:Gnome-colors-list-remove.svg|20px|alt=removed|Removed]] [[Special:PermaLink/1052942640#Wikipedia:Inactive administrators/2021#November 2021|A Train]] • [[Special:PermaLink/1052942640#Wikipedia:Inactive administrators/2021#November 2021|Berean Hunter]] • [[Special:PermaLink/1056156505#Level 1 desysop of Epbr123|Epbr123]] • [[Special:PermaLink/1052942640#Wikipedia:Inactive administrators/2021#November 2021|GermanJoe]] • [[Special:PermaLink/1056716258#Voluntarily_resigning_my_administrative_permissions|Sanchom]] • [[Special:PermaLink/1057799750#Resigning admin permissions|Mysid]] [[File:Octicons-tools.svg|20px|alt=]] '''Technical news''' :* Unregistered editors using the mobile website are now able to receive notices to indicate they have talk page messages. The notice looks similar to what is already present on desktop, and will be displayed on when viewing any page except mainspace and when editing any page. ({{phab|T284642}}) :* The limit on the number of emails a user can send per day has been made global instead of per-wiki to help prevent abuse. ({{phab|T293866}}) [[File:Scale of justice 2.svg|20px|alt=]] '''Arbitration''' :* Voting in the [[Wikipedia:Arbitration Committee Elections December 2021|2021 Arbitration Committee Elections]] is open until 23:59, 06 December 2021 (UTC). :* The already authorized standard [[WP:AC/DS|discretionary sanctions]] for all pages relating to the [[Horn of Africa]] (defined as including Ethiopia, Somalia, Eritrea, Djibouti, and adjoining areas if involved in related disputes), broadly construed, [[Special:Permalink/1057777898#Arbitration_motion_regarding_Horn_of_Africa|have been made permanent]]. ---- {{Center|{{Flatlist| * [[Wikipedia talk:Administrators' newsletter|Discuss this newsletter]] * [[Wikipedia:Administrators' newsletter/Subscribe|Subscribe]] * [[Wikipedia:Administrators' newsletter/Archive|Archive]] }}}} {{center|1=Sent by [[User:MediaWiki message delivery|MediaWiki message delivery]] ([[User talk:MediaWiki message delivery|talk]]) 17:25, 3 December 2021 (UTC)}} == [[m:Special:MyLanguage/Tech/News/2021/49|Tech News: 2021-49]] ==
Latest '''[[m:Special:MyLanguage/Tech/News|tech news]]''' from the Wikimedia technical community. Please tell other users about these changes. Not all changes will affect you. [[m:Special:MyLanguage/Tech/News/2021/49|Translations]] are available. '''Problems''' * MediaWiki 1.38-wmf.11 was scheduled to be deployed on some wikis last week. The deployment was delayed because of unexpected problems. '''Changes later this week''' * [[File:Octicons-sync.svg|12px|link=|Recurrent item]] The [[mw:MediaWiki 1.38/wmf.12|new version]] of MediaWiki will be on test wikis and MediaWiki.org from {{#time:j xg|2021-12-07|en}}. It will be on non-Wikipedia wikis and some Wikipedias from {{#time:j xg|2021-12-08|en}}. It will be on all wikis from {{#time:j xg|2021-12-09|en}} ([[mw:MediaWiki 1.38/Roadmap|calendar]]). * At all Wikipedias, a Mentor Dashboard is now available at Special:MentorDashboard. It allows registered mentors, who take care of newcomers' first steps, to monitor their assigned newcomers' activity. It is part of the [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Growth/Feature summary|Growth features]]. You can learn more about [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Growth/Communities/How_to_configure_the_mentors%27_list|activating the mentor list]] on your wiki and about [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Growth/Mentor dashboard|the mentor dashboard project]]. * [[File:Octicons-tools.svg|15px|link=|Advanced item]] The predecessor to the current [[mw:API|MediaWiki Action API]] (which was created in 2008), action=ajax, will be removed this week. Any scripts or bots using it will need to switch to the corresponding API module. [https://phabricator.wikimedia.org/T42786] * [[File:Octicons-tools.svg|15px|link=|Advanced item]] An old ResourceLoader module, jquery.jStorage, which was deprecated in 2016, will be removed this week. Any scripts or bots using it will need to switch to mediawiki.storage instead. Latest '''[[m:Special:MyLanguage/Tech/News|tech news]]''' from the Wikimedia technical community. Please tell other users about these changes. Not all changes will affect you. [[m:Special:MyLanguage/Tech/News/2021/51|Translations]] are available. '''Tech News''' * Because of the [[w:en:Christmas and holiday season|holidays]] the next issue of Tech News will be sent out on 10 January 2022. '''Recent changes''' * Queries made by the DynamicPageList extension () are now only allowed to run for 10 seconds and error if they take longer. This is in response to multiple outages where long-running queries caused an outage on all wikis. [https://phabricator.wikimedia.org/T287380#7575719] '''Changes later this week''' * There is no new MediaWiki version this week or next week. '''Future changes''' * The developers of the Wikipedia iOS app are looking for testers who edit in multiple languages. You can [[mw:Wikimedia Apps/Team/iOS/202112 testing|read more and let them know if you are interested]]. * [[File:Octicons-tools.svg|15px|link=|Advanced item]] The Wikimedia [[wikitech:Portal:Cloud VPS|Cloud VPS]] hosts technical projects for the Wikimedia movement. Developers need to [[wikitech:News/Cloud VPS 2021 Purge|claim projects]] they use. This is because old and unused projects are removed once a year. Unclaimed projects can be shut down from February. 22:04, 20 December 2021 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Me an u wow|Me an u wow]] on [[Help:Getting started]] (04:42, 23 December 2021) == Hello --[[User:Me an u wow|Me an u wow]] ([[User talk:Me an u wow|talk]]) 04:42, 23 December 2021 (UTC) == Scripts++ Newsletter – Issue 22 ==
Hello everyone, and welcome to the 22nd issue of the Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Scripts++|Scripts++ Newsletter]]. This issue will be covering new and updated user scripts from the past seven months (June through December 2021). [[File:Farm-Fresh scripts text.png|20px|alt=Scripts]] Got anything good? Tell us about your new, improved, old, or messed-up script [[Wikipedia talk:Scripts++|here]]! {{Col-begin|width=auto}} {{Col-break}} [[File:Cscr-featured.svg|20px|alt=Featured script|This month's featured script]] '''Featured script''' : '''[[User:Alexis Jazz/LuckyRename|LuckyRename]]''', by '''[[User:Alexis Jazz|Alexis Jazz]]''', is this month's featured script. LuckyRename makes requesting file moves easier, and automates the many steps in file moving (including automatic replacement of existing usage). Give it a shot! [[File:Gnome-colors-view-refresh.svg|20px|alt=Updated scripts|Updated]] '''Updated scripts''' * '''''[[User:SD0001|SD0001]]''''': '''[[User:SD0001/hide-reverted-edits|hide-reverted-edits]]''' has been updated to take into account changes in reversion tools like Twinkle and RedWarn. * '''''[[User:Writ Keeper|Writ Keeper]]''''': '''[[User:Writ Keeper/Scripts/massRollback.js|massRollback.js]]''' has added a rollback-some option (missed in last issue). * '''''[[User:ClaudineChionh|ClaudineChionh]]''''': '''[[User:ClaudineChionh/Scripts/SkinSwitcher|SkinSwitcher]]''' (a fork and update of [[User:Eizen|Eizen]]'s [[User:Eizen/SkinSwitcher|script]]) provides an options menu/toolbox/toolbar allowing users to view a given page in MediaWiki's default skins. {{Col-break|gap=10em}} [[File:Text-x-source.svg|240px|right|alt=Scripts++ Newsletter|Scripts++ Newsletter]] [[File:1009Clock.jpg|20px|alt=Pending requests]] '''Pending requests''' * Mass-patrolling at [[Special:NewPages]] * Taking up the mantle of an almost completed peer review javascript bot * Restore the Wikipedia Citation Tool for Google Books * Autohighlighting specific characters (Greek) * ...and many more, all available at [[Wikipedia:User scripts/Requests]] [[File:Info Simple bw.svg|20px|alt=Miscellaneous]] '''Miscellaneous''' * '''[[Wikipedia:User scripts/Ranking]]''' is a sortable table of Wikipedia's thousand-or-so most commonly used scripts; it includes their author, last modification date, installation count, and sometimes a short description. * '''[[:m:Toolhub|Toolhub]]''' is a community managed catalog of software tools used in the Wikimedia movement. Technical volunteers can use Toolhub to document the tools that they create or maintain. All Wikimedians can use Toolhub to search for tools to help with their workflows and to create lists of useful tools to share with others. {{Col-end}} [[File:Gnome-colors-list-add.svg|20px|alt=New scripts|New]] '''New scripts''' *'''''[[User:Ahecht|Ahecht]]''''': **'''[[User:Ahecht/Scripts/draft-sorter|draft-sorter]]''' sorts AfC drafts by adding WikiProject banners to their talk pages. It supersedes [[User:Enterprisey/draft-sorter]], adding a few features and fixing some bugs. **'''[[User:Ahecht/Scripts/massmove|massmove]]''', a modified [[User:Plastikspork/massmove.js]] that adds a link to the left column, allows adding and removing both prefixes and suffixes. **'''[[User:Ahecht/Scripts/watchlistcleaner|watchlistcleaner]]''' removes missing pages (redlinks), redirects, pages you haven't edited recently, and/or pages you've never edited from your watchlist. *'''''[[User:Awesome Aasim|Awesome Aasim]]''''': **'''[[User:Awesome Aasim/infiniscroll|Infiniscroll]]''' adds infinite scrolling to user contributions, page histories, and log pages. **'''[[User:Awesome Aasim/quickcreate|Quick create]]''' allows for the fast creation of red-linked pages with two clicks. *'''''[[User:Caburum|Caburum]]''''': **'''[[User:Caburum/UTCclock|UTCclock]]''' adds a clock displaying the current UTC time. *'''''[[User:Chlod|Chlod]]''''': **'''[[User:Chlod/Scripts/CopiedTemplateEditor|CopiedTemplateEditor]]''', mainly for [[WP:CCI|CCI]] case handlers, allows graphically editing a talk page's {{tl|copied}} templates. *'''''[[User:DaxServer|DaxServer]]''''': **'''[[User:DaxServer/BooksToSfn|BooksToSfn]]''' adds a portlet link in [[WP:VE|Visual Editor]]'s source mode editing, in main namespace articles or in the user's Sandbox. When clicked, it converts one {{tlx|cite book}} inside a ... tag block into an {{tlx|Sfn}}. *'''''[[User:FlightTime|FlightTime]]''''': **'''[[User:FlightTime/OneClickArchiver|OneClickArchiver]]''' is a custom version of [[User:Technical_13/Scripts/OneClickArchiver]] which doesn't prepend {{Tl|Clear}} to the top of each section on the archive page. *'''''[[m:User:Jon Harald Søby|Jon Harald Søby]]''''': **'''[[m:User:Jon Harald Søby/diffedit|diffedit]]''' enables editing directly from viewing a diff "when, for instance, you notice a tiny mistake deep into an article, and don't want to edit the entire article and re-find that one line to fix that tiny mistake". **'''[[c:User:Jon Harald Søby/warnOnLargeFile.js|warnOnLargeFile]]''' warns you if you're about to open a very large file (width/height >10,000px or file size >100 MB) from a file page. *'''''[[User:JPxG|JPxG]]''''': **'''[https://github.com/jp-x-g/PressPass PressPass]''' adds a collection of tools for Newspapers.com including configurable automatic citation generation in five different formats. **'''[[User:JPxG/current-switcher.js|CurrentSwitcher]]''' gives you links on the contribs page to hide duplicate entries, current revisions, rollbacks, huggles, twinkles, and redwarns. ** '''[[User:JPxG/TrackSum.js|TrackSum]]''' lets you automatically sum the lengths of tracks in templates like {{tl|track listing}} and get total runtimes. *'''''[[User:Nardog|Nardog]]''''': **'''[[User:Nardog/CopySectLink|CopySectLink]]''' adds a button to copy the unencoded page title or section path next to each heading. **'''[[User:Nardog/IPAInput|IPAInput]]''' allows you to type in IPA symbols by directly looking at an IPA key like [[Help:IPA/English]] and clicking on the symbols. **'''[[User:Nardog/TemplatePreviewGuard|TemplatePreviewGuard]]''' warns when you try to use "Preview page with this template" with a page that doesn't transclude the template. *'''''[[User:NguoiDungKhongDinhDanh|NguoiDungKhongDinhDanh]]''''': **'''[[User:NguoiDungKhongDinhDanh/ContribsTabVector.js|ContribsTabVector]]''' adds "Contributions" and "Statistics" tabs to user and user talk pages on the [[WP:VECTOR|Vector]] skin. **'''[[User:NguoiDungKhongDinhDanh/CopyvioChecker|CopyvioChecker]]''' adds a "CopyvioCheck" tab to all pages, except [[Help:Special page|Special]] (Vector skin only). **'''[[User:NguoiDungKhongDinhDanh/LiveDiffLink.js|LiveDiffLink]]''' is a version of [[User:Equazcion/LiveDiffLink|Equazcion's LiveDiffLink]] which shows a wikilink instead of a URL. **'''[[m:User:NguoiDungKhongDinhDanh/QuickDiff|QuickDiff]]''' (by OneTwoThreeFall at Fandom) lets you quickly view any diff link on a wiki, whether on Recent Changes, contribs pages, history pages, the diff view itself, or elsewhere. For more information, view its [https://dev.fandom.com/wiki/QuickDiff page on Fandom]. *'''''[[User:Novem Linguae|Novem Linguae]]''''': **'''[[User:Novem Linguae/Scripts/DetectSNG.js|DetectSNG]]''' scans a list of 1,600 [[WP:SNG|SNG]] keywords and displays them at the top of the article. **'''[[User:Novem Linguae/Scripts/NotSoFast.js|NotSoFast]]''' highlights recently created articles in the new pages feed, to discourage patrolling them too quickly. **'''[[User:Novem Linguae/Scripts/UserRightsDiff.js|UserRightsDiff]]''' concisely displays what perm was added or removed when viewing [[Special:UserRights]]. **'''[[User:Novem Linguae/Scripts/VoteCounter.js|VoteCounter]]''' displays a rough count of keeps and deletes at XFDs, RFCs, etc. **'''[[User:Novem Linguae/Scripts/WatchlistAFD.js|WatchlistAFD]]''' automatically watchlists the [[WP:AFD|AFDs]] of any pages you [[WP:AFC|AFC]] accept or [[WP:NPP|NPP]] patrol, to help you calibrate your reviewing. *'''''[[User:P.T.Đ|P.T.Đ]]''''': **'''[[User:P.T.Đ/TwinkleMobile|TwinkleMobile]]''' enables Twinkle on mobile view (Minerva skin). *'''''[[User:Qwerfjkl|Qwerfjkl]]''''': **'''[[User:Qwerfjkl/scripts/editRedirect|editRedirect]]''' adds a → link after redirects to edit them. **'''[[User:Qwerfjkl/scripts/RETF|RegExTypoFix]]''', a script for fixing typos, is a wrapper for [[User:Joeytje50/RETF.js]]. **'''[[User:Qwerfjkl/scripts/talkback|talkback]]''' creates links after user talk page links like this: |{{False link|C}}|{{False link|TB}} (with the first linking to the user's contributions, and the latter giving the option of sending a {{tl|talkback}} notice). It also adds a [{{False link|copy}}] link next to section headers. *'''''[[User:Rublov|Rublov]]''''': **'''[[User:Rublov/diff-link|diff-link]]''' shows "copy" links on history and contributions pages that copy an internal link to the diff (e.g., [[Special:Diff/1026402230]]) to your clipboard when clicked. *'''''[[User:Rummskartoffel|Rummskartoffel]]''''': **'''[[User:Rummskartoffel/auto-watchlist-expiry|auto-watchlist-expiry]]''' automatically watchlists every page you edit for a user-definable duration (you can still pick a different time using the dropdown, though). **'''[[User:Rummskartoffel/generate pings|generate pings]]''' generates the wikitext needed to ping all members of a category, up to 50 editors (the limit defined by MediaWiki). **'''[[User:Rummskartoffel/share ExpandTemplates url|share ExpandTemplates url]]''' allows for easy sharing of your inputs to [[Special:ExpandTemplates]]. It adds a button that, when clicked, copies a shareable URL to your exact invocation of the page, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:ExpandTemplates?wpContextTitle=John+Doe+%28Wikipedia+editor%29&wpInput=%7B%7B%23invoke%3AExample%7Chello%7D%7D&wpRemoveComments=1&wpRemoveNowiki=0&wpGenerateXml=1&wpGenerateRawHtml=0 like this]. Other editors '''do not''' need to have this script installed in order to access the URL generated. **'''[[User:Rummskartoffel/show tag names|show tag names]]''' shows the real names of tags next to their display names in places such as page revision histories or the watchlist. *'''''[[User:Tol|Tol]]''''': **'''[[User:Tol/VECN|VisualEditor Citation Needed]]''' adds a button (under "Insert") in VisualEditor to add a {{tl|citation needed}} tag. *'''''[[User:Venkat TL|Venkat TL]]''''': **'''[[User:Venkat TL/ColourContrib|ColourContrib]]''' color-codes the user contributions page so that pages you've edited last are sharply distinguished from pages where another editor was the last to edit the page. *'''''[[User:Vukky|Vukky]]''''': **'''[[User:Vukky/Scripts/StatusChanger|StatusChanger]]''' is a fork of [[User:Enterprisey/StatusChanger.js|Enterprisey's Status Changer]], which adds a UI to the script. (using [[Wikipedia:Morebits|Morebits]], so you'll need to have [[WP:TW|Twinkle]] enabled to use it). All in all, some very neat scripts were written in these last few months. Hoping to see many more in the next issue -- [[Wikipedia talk:Scripts++|drop us a line on the talk page]] if you've been writing (or seeing) anything cool and good. Filling in for DannyS712, this has been '''[[User:JPxG|jp]]'''×'''[[User talk:JPxG|g]]'''. Take care, and merry Christmas! '''[[User:JPxG|jp]]'''×'''[[User talk:JPxG|g]]''' 07:30, 24 December 2021 (UTC) ---- {{center|{{flatlist| * [[Wikipedia:Scripts++|About the newsletter]] * [[Wikipedia:Scripts++/Archive|Archive]] * [[Wikipedia:Scripts++/Subscribe|Subscribe or unsubscribe]] * [[Wikipedia talk:Scripts++/Issue 22|Discuss this issue]] }}}}

== Question from [[User:Sairam L|Sairam L]] on [[User:Sairam L]] (19:09, 8 November 2021) == Hello How to add in image --[[User:Sairam L|Sairam L]] ([[User talk:Sairam L|talk]]) 19:09, 8 November 2021 (UTC) :[[User:Sairam L|Sairam L]], hello and welcome to Wikipedia! If you already have an image on Wikipedia that you want to use and you know its file name, you can add it to an article like this:
[[File:MyImage.png|thumb|caption]] (where MyImage.png is the file name of the image, and caption is the image's caption.)
If you want to upload an image, you can use the [[Wikipedia:File Upload Wizard|File Upload Wizard]], then use the image as explained above. Happy editing! ― [[User:Vukky|Vukky]][[User talk:Vukky|Talk]][[User:Vukky/Guestbook|Guestbook]] 19:22, 8 November 2021 (UTC) == Question from [[User:JonahFineArt|JonahFineArt]] (18:55, 9 November 2021) == Hello. I am trying to create a biography page for an artist by the name of Eliot Thompson. He is an under appreciated artist who worked with many of the important Washington DC Color School artists and his work is starting to be discovered. I was hoping to have an extensive biography out there for people to see and learn about the artist. There are some wonderful articles and history that can just be consolidated and sited for the biography. Any help you can offer me is greatly appreciated. --[[User:JonahFineArt|JonahFineArt]] ([[User talk:JonahFineArt|talk]]) 18:55, 9 November 2021 (UTC) == ArbCom 2021 Elections voter message ==
[[File:Scale of justice 2.svg|40px]]Hello! Voting in the '''[[WP:ACE2021|2021 Arbitration Committee elections]]''' is now open until 23:59 (UTC) on {{#time:l, j F Y|{{Arbitration Committee candidate/data|2021|end}}-1 day}}. All '''[[Wikipedia:Arbitration Committee Elections December 2021#Election timeline|eligible users]]''' are allowed to vote. Users with alternate accounts may only vote once. The [[WP:ARBCOM|Arbitration Committee]] is the panel of editors responsible for conducting the [[Wikipedia:Arbitration|Wikipedia arbitration process]]. It has the authority to impose binding solutions to disputes between editors, primarily for serious conduct disputes the community has been unable to resolve. This includes the authority to impose [[WP:BAN|site bans]], [[WP:TBAN|topic bans]], editing restrictions, and other measures needed to maintain our editing environment. The [[Wikipedia:Arbitration/Policy|arbitration policy]] describes the Committee's roles and responsibilities in greater detail. If you wish to participate in the 2021 election, please review [[Wikipedia:Arbitration Committee Elections December 2021/Candidates|the candidates]] and submit your choices on the '''[[Special:SecurePoll/vote/{{Arbitration Committee candidate/data|2021|poll}}|voting page]]'''. If you no longer wish to receive these messages, you may add {{tlx|NoACEMM}} to your user talk page. Latest '''[[m:Special:MyLanguage/Tech/News|tech news]]''' from the Wikimedia technical community. Please tell other users about these changes. Not all changes will affect you. [[m:Special:MyLanguage/Tech/News/2022/05|Translations]] are available. '''Recent changes''' * [[File:Octicons-tools.svg|15px|link=|alt=|Advanced item]] If a gadget should support the new ?withgadget URL parameter that was [[m:Special:MyLanguage/Tech/News/2022/02|announced]] 3 weeks ago, then it must now also specify supportsUrlLoad in the gadget definition ([[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Extension:Gadgets#supportsUrlLoad|documentation]]). [https://phabricator.wikimedia.org/T29766] '''Changes later this week''' * [[File:Octicons-sync.svg|12px|link=|alt=|Recurrent item]] The [[mw:MediaWiki 1.38/wmf.20|new version]] of MediaWiki will be on test wikis and MediaWiki.org from {{#time:j xg|2022-02-01|en}}. It will be on non-Wikipedia wikis and some Wikipedias from {{#time:j xg|2022-02-02|en}}. It will be on all wikis from {{#time:j xg|2022-02-03|en}} ([[mw:MediaWiki 1.38/Roadmap|calendar]]). '''Future changes''' * A change that was [[m:Special:MyLanguage/Tech/News/2021/16|announced]] last year was delayed. It is now ready to move ahead: ** The user group oversight will be renamed suppress. This is for [[phab:T109327|technical reasons]]. This is the technical name. It doesn't affect what you call the editors with this user right on your wiki. This is planned to happen in three weeks. You can comment [[phab:T112147|in Phabricator]] if you have objections. As usual, these labels can be translated on translatewiki ([[phab:T112147|direct links are available]]) or by administrators on your wiki. '''Events''' * You can vote on proposals in the [[m:Special:MyLanguage/Community Wishlist Survey 2022|Community Wishlist Survey]] between 28 January and 11 February. The survey decides what the [[m:Special:MyLanguage/Community Tech|Community Tech team]] will work on. Latest '''[[m:Special:MyLanguage/Tech/News|tech news]]''' from the Wikimedia technical community. Please tell other users about these changes. Not all changes will affect you. [[m:Special:MyLanguage/Tech/News/2022/07|Translations]] are available. '''Recent changes''' * [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Manual:Purge|Purging]] a category page with fewer than 5,000 members will now recount it completely. This will allow editors to fix incorrect counts when it is wrong. [https://phabricator.wikimedia.org/T85696] '''Changes later this week''' * [[File:Octicons-sync.svg|12px|link=|alt=|Recurrent item]] The [[mw:MediaWiki 1.38/wmf.22|new version]] of MediaWiki will be on test wikis and MediaWiki.org from {{#time:j xg|2022-02-15|en}}. It will be on non-Wikipedia wikis and some Wikipedias from {{#time:j xg|2022-02-16|en}}. It will be on all wikis from {{#time:j xg|2022-02-17|en}} ([[mw:MediaWiki 1.38/Roadmap|calendar]]). * [[File:Octicons-tools.svg|15px|link=|Advanced item]] In the [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Extension:AbuseFilter|AbuseFilter]] extension, the rmspecials() function has been updated so that it does not remove the "space" character. Wikis are advised to wrap all the uses of rmspecials() with rmwhitespace() wherever necessary to keep filters' behavior unchanged. You can use the search function on [[Special:AbuseFilter]] to locate its usage.
